en: If anyone asks, tell them it was bestowed by the Imperator of Amphoreus. Heh, and if they want proof, let them come and seek an audience with me themselves.

ch: 我曾梦想做个棋士，四处云游，以棋会友…可惜翁法罗斯不能一日无君，这个想法也就一直被我搁置了。
en: I once dreamed of being a chess master, traveling the world, and making friends through the game... But alas, Amphoreus can't go a day without its ruler, so that idea has been shelved away.

ch: 呵呵，我也没想到，当年嚷嚷着要去悬锋城学剑的你，有一天竟然能击败尼卡多利！
en: Heh, who would've thought? Back then, you were all about "wanting to learn swordsmanship at Castrum Kremnos!" And now look at you, you've actually beaten Nikador!

ch: 怎么会，我想念哀丽秘榭的菜好久好久了！
en: No way, I've been craving Aedes Elysiae's food for ages!

ch: 替老爸砍柴火，为伽尔巴叔叔制弓，和皮西厄斯老师讲讲树庭的见闻…还有闹着让我讲故事的披索和莉维娅，要满足他们的好奇心，可得费些功夫。
en: Chopping firewood for Dad, making bows for Uncle Galba, sharing tales from the Grove with Mrs. Pythias... And then there's Piso and Livia, always pestering me for stories. It takes quite some effort to satisfy their curiosity.

ch: 对了，还得去探望迷路迷境的妖精们…许久不见，它们恐怕又稀里糊涂地把日子过得一团糟了吧？
en: Oh right, I should also visit the fairies in the Membrance Maze... It's been a while. I bet they've made a complete mess of things again in their usual scatterbrained way?

ch: 老友，听你说起这些事情，好像回到了从前结伴征战的岁月里。不过，现在的翁法罗斯，再也不需要战争啦！
en: Old friend, hearing you talk about these things brings me back to the days when we fought side by side. But now, Amphoreus has no need for war anymore!

ch: 吉奥刻勒斯他，常常为从前的那桩背叛痛心，对那位奥赫玛的君王——刻律德菈，态度总是有些别扭。
en: Geocles is often heartbroken over that betrayal from the past, and his attitude toward the ruler of Okhema, Cerydra, has always been somewhat awkward.

ch: 翁法罗斯的持明卵？
en: An Amphorean Vidyadhara egg?

ch: 倘若有一天它能孵化，从中诞生的新生命，或许是翁法罗斯第一个真正步入银河的生命吧？
en: If it hatches one day, perhaps the new life born from within will be the first Amphorean life to truly step into the cosmos?

ch: 敬翁法罗斯！
en: To Amphoreus!

ch: 我只是借此机会考验一下你的医术罢了。可别太得意忘形哦，树庭女孩。
en: I was just taking the chance to test your medical skills. Don't get too full of yourself, Grove girl.

ch: 劳你费心啰，树庭女孩！
en: Thanks, Grove girl!

ch: 希望铭记的东西…呀，看灰宝期盼的表情，是想要翁法罗斯纪念品，对吧？
en: Something to remember...? Oh, judging by Grayie's eager expression, you want an Amphoreus souvenir, right?

ch: 听说荒笛也在树庭仰望过「星空」，遮蔽双眼的永昼辉光退去后，天外真正的星空依旧令人着迷。
en: I heard that even Terravox once gazed at the stars in the Grove. After the blinding radiance of Eternal Day faded, the true starry sky beyond is still mesmerizing.

ch: 呜嗡…嚼嚼…翁法罗斯万物生灵现在都有了自己的归宿，我也可以安心享受美味的红土了。
en: Grumph... Nomnom... Now that all the Amphorean creatures have found their places, I can finally enjoy my delicious redsoil in peace.

ch: 翁法罗斯的持明卵？
en: An Amphorean Vidyadhara egg?

ch: 倘若有一天它能孵化，从中诞生的新生命，或许是翁法罗斯第一个真正步入银河的生命吧？
en: If it hatches one day, perhaps the new life born from within will be the first Amphorean life to truly step into the cosmos?

ch: 那么——敬翁法罗斯的明天。
en: Well then, here's to Amphoreus's tomorrow.

ch: 汝计划以悬锋为饵，可自城邦陷落后，那些厮杀反而成为滋养疯狂的养料。
en: You planned to use Kremnos as bait, but after the city fell, those massacres only became sustenance for madness.

ch: 你说得对，罗浮杂俎账号名为「云璃一生应援」的先生。
en: Whatever you say, He-Whose-Ghostly Grove-Account-Is-Named-"YunliFanForLife."

ch: 你对翁法罗斯了解多少？
en: How much do you know about Amphoreus?

ch: 关于翁法罗斯…
en: Regarding Amphoreus...

ch: 打捞湮没无闻的「记忆」是我的职责，而翁法罗斯…就像橱窗中的珍宝，星神的明光闪烁其间，令人沉醉不已。
en: It is my duty to salvage memories that are forgotten, while Amphoreus... is a treasure behind a window, with the Aeons' blinding light radiating from within, mesmerizing all.

ch: 你对翁法罗斯了解多少？
en: How much do you know about Amphoreus?

ch: 你久久地望着那道创口，仿佛置身于炙热的阳炎照亮树庭的一刹那，似乎还有一声呐喊：「我焚烧一切，只为净化这虚伪的黄金！」
en: You gaze at the wound for what feels like an eternity, as if caught in that moment when scorching sunlight illuminated the Grove. And there was seemingly a bellow: "I shall incinerate everything, all to purify this False Chrysos!"

ch: 你是不是以为我在胡说八道？很遗憾，在翁法罗斯发现「光线通过胶体的散射现象」的树庭学者，就叫达利原。
en: You think I'm talking nonsense? Well, hate to break it to you, but the Grove scholar who discovered "light scattering through colloids" in Amphoreus was literally named Dall.

ch: 我们离开奥赫玛吧…
en: Let's leave Okhema...

ch: 我是伊阿宋，目前管理着这支商队「阿尔戈」。奥赫玛的大地兽都是我的好朋友，它们也是多亏了我才有自己的名字。
en: I'm Iason, the manager of this caravan "Argo." All dromas in Okhema are my good friends, and I'm the one who gave each of them a name.

ch: 最近世道挺乱，你要是有什么东西想从外面运进奥赫玛来，或者就是想买一些稀奇物件，找我们「阿尔戈」下单，那是最安全的。赫拉克勒斯像黄金裔一样值得信赖！
en: Things aren't as calm as they used to be. If you need to move anything into Okhema or find something rare, Argo's your safest bet. Trust me, Heracles is just as dependable as the Chrysos Heirs!

ch: 某日我望见在城里巡逻的白厄大人，灵光一闪将奥赫玛黄金裔们的英雄事迹绘制在了陶罐之上。
en: As for their origin, one day, while spotting Lord Phainon patrolling the city, I felt inspired and painted the epic tales of Okhema's Chrysos Heirs on a dolium.

ch: 翁法罗斯能撑到那天吗？
en: Will Amphoreus exist until then?

ch: 我当然还是希望翁法罗斯存续，这就要拜托各位黄金裔大人了。
en: But then again, I do hope Amphoreus endures — a responsibility that rests on all esteemed Chrysos Heirs.

ch: 可承住我理想的创作，并非如今大家认可我的原因…人们只记得我常为奥赫玛的名流作画，也只爱我笔下的黄金裔。
en: But people do not currently acknowledge me due to the creations that bear my true dream... People remember me mostly for my paintings of the upper echelon of Okhema and my portrayal of the Chrysos Heirs.

ch: …上一世，我是预言中翁法罗斯的救世主。我的降生给所有瑟希斯的信徒带来希望，树庭上下倾尽所有将我养育至预言中对抗黑潮的那一天。
en: ...In my previous life, I was the prophesied savior of Amphoreus. My birth instilled hope in every faithful disciple of Cerces, with the Grove sparing no expense in bringing me up for the day when I am prophesied to stand against the encroaching black tide.

ch: 这个白麻布是整个奥赫玛应用最广泛的布料。走在街上你能看见的十个人里，有九个都穿它制成的衣服呢。
en: White linen is a staple fabric in Okhema. It's everywhere — chances are, nine out of ten people you see are wearing clothes made from it.

ch: 我就知道你们这些本地人根本瞧不上外邦的流民！假惺惺地做给谁看呢？什么奥赫玛文化传统，我看被历史淘汰的糟粕还差不多！
en: I knew you locals wouldn't treat us outlanders with respect! Who are you trying to impress? Talking about Okhema's culture and history? Hah, sounds more like trash destined to be forgotten by time!

ch: 这个嘛，「躺食」其实是奥赫玛的一种传统习俗。在遥远的黄金世，人们曾认为侧躺在椅子上进食，能够更好地与大地泰坦产生共鸣。
en: Well, "reclining dining" is actually an old tradition here in Okhema. Back in the ancient Era Chrysea, people believed that reclining on chairs while dining helped them resonate with the Earth Titan.

ch: 最后是甜点——整个奥赫玛最新鲜的苹果，配上我改良过的肉桂蜂蜜酱，刚好适合为这一餐做个完美的收尾。
en: Finally, we've got dessert — the freshest apples in Okhema paired with my upgraded cinnamon honey sauce, a delightful end to your meal.

ch: 由于黑潮的缘故，这些年陆续有不少外邦人移居奥赫玛。我有幸从他们那得到了一些食谱，并结合其历史文化进行了复刻与适当的改良。这些是我比较满意的作品——
en: Over the years, many outlanders have made their home in Okhema due to the black tide. I've been fortunate enough to get some of their recipes and put my own spin on them based on the relevant history and cultures. Here are a couple of my favorites...

ch: 所以我用奥赫玛本地的奶浆果作为代替品——就结果来看，这个尝试还是挺成功的，哈哈。
en: Therefore, I used our milkberries in Okhema instead, and it was a pretty successful attempt, if I do say so myself!

ch: 你当时正准备越过悬锋城，对吧？没想到闯进了奥赫玛军队的埋伏，和我们一样。还有旁边这个{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}信徒也是。
en: You were just about to cross Castrum Kremnos, right? You didn't expect to get into an ambush by the forces of Okhema, just like us and this believer of {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} over there.

ch: 翁法罗斯也能有模因污染？
en: Can Amphoreus even get memetic pollution?

ch: {NICKNAME}退化——奥赫玛的双耳陶瓶！
en: {NICKNAME} regresses — an Okhema twin-eared vase!

ch: 欢迎来到奥赫玛，年轻人，对这边的生活还适应吗？
en: Welcome to Okhema, young one. How are you finding life here so far?

ch: 哈哈，初来乍到时大家多少都会有些不适应。不过要是待得久了，你就会发现奥赫玛还是挺宜居的——无论气候、环境还是生活节奏。
en: Aha, it can take some time for everyone to get used to things at first, but once you do, you'll see that Okhema is quite cozy — that goes for the weather, the surroundings, and the lifestyle.

ch: 哈哈，那真是太好了。作为受到刻法勒庇佑的圣城，奥赫玛并没有受到黑潮的影响，无论气候、环境还是生活节奏都非常宜人。
en: Aha! That's great to hear! This holy city, blessed by Kephale, remains unharmed by the black tide. Over time, you'll find that Okhema is quite cozy — that goes for the weather, the surroundings, and the lifestyle.

ch: 说来黄金裔中那位万敌阁下就来自悬锋城，他偶尔路过这里时，总会买些石榴。托他的福，这种水果的销量一直不错。
en: Speaking of which, Lord Mydeimos among the Chrysos Heirs hails from Castrum Kremnos. He always picks up some pomegranates whenever he swings by. Thanks to him, the sales of pomegranates have been thriving.

ch: 诶呀，看起来你应该会和那位万敌阁下非常有共同语言呢。承蒙惠顾，<unbreak>100</unbreak>利衡币。
en: Oh, it looks like you and Lord Mydeimos share a similar taste. That'll be <unbreak>100</unbreak> Balance Coins. Thank you for your patronage!

ch: 我会，保卫奥赫玛的安全，不保卫的时候，我会做饭。
en: I'll be protecting Okhema, and when I'm not, I'll be cooking.

ch: 我以前，也是异乡人。刚来奥赫玛，元老院的移民官，问我以前做什么。我说，是厨师，是高级厨师。
en: I was an outlander too. When I came to Okhema, the immigration officer from the Council of Elders asked me what I did, and I told them I was a cook, and a good one.

ch: 奥赫玛的菜色，普通，但是诚实。想起以前见过，信仰扎格列斯的人，卖粥。
en: Okhema's food is simple but honest. I remember meeting a believer of Zagreus who sold congee.

ch: 我到过，悬锋城。在那边做饭，很累。
en: I've been to Castrum Kremnos. Cooking there is tiring.

ch: 哈哈，倒也不必较真！俗话说，「矫枉必须过正」——要是没有我们站在风俗的另一端，圣城奥赫玛，恐怕难免要变得过于神圣…那住着多难受啊？
en: Haha, no need to get all serious! Like they say, "when you push something, you gotta push it far." Without us standing on the other side of morality's scales, the holy city Okhema would become far too holy... and that'd be a pain to live in, don't you think?

ch: 不过，你也不用太担心；只要黄金裔还打算将泰坦取而代之，只要普通人里还有像我这样的冒牌信徒，奥赫玛的气氛就还不至于难以忍受…如果他们声称自己信的是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}就更好了！
en: But hey, don't stress too much. As long as there are Chrysos Heirs aiming to overthrow and become Titans, and there are still ordinary people like me pretending to be devout, Okhema's vibe won't be too suffocating... especially if they claim to worship {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}.

ch: 实话说，上次我们的集会就被遐蝶小姐劝阻了，说奥赫玛再怎么样也是一座圣城，不可以这样无事生非、小题大作——她人虽然温柔，但那种生人勿近的感觉还是挺可怕的！
en: To be honest, Castorice dissuaded us at our previous gatherings, saying that Okhema is still a holy city no matter what, and we can't stir up drama like this. While she's gentle most of the time, she can be intimidating with how aloof she is at times!

ch: 总之，多亏了这是个承认遗产合法性的城邦…在「再创世」到来之前，只要奥赫玛还没有在黑潮中陷落，我就能无拘无束地生活下去。想睡就睡，想吃就吃，想晒太阳就晒太阳——多好。
en: Anyway, thanks to this city's acceptance of legal inheritance... I can lead a carefree life until the Era Nova, as long as Okhema remains steadfast against the black tide. I sleep, eat, and soak up the sun whenever I please... What a bliss.

ch: 对于那些笃信泰坦的人来说，{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}无疑是最可怕的神明。人们说祂催生黑潮，要把毁灭带给{RUBY_B#世界}翁法罗斯{RUBY_E#}。
en: For devout disciples of the Titans, the {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} is definitely a horrifying deity. They believe they are the mastermind behind the black tide, destined to bring catastrophe upon {RUBY_B#the world}Amphoreus{RUBY_E#}.

ch: 这么说吧…如果你在奥赫玛听谁说自己笃信塞纳托斯，那么有很大的可能，这人只是不愿对泰坦顶礼膜拜，或者懒得参加任何祈求赐福的仪式。
en: Listen... If someone in Okhema declares themselves a follower of Thanatos, it's likely because they just prefer not to worship the Titans or partake in any rituals that beg for blessings.

ch: 留给{RUBY_B#旧世界}翁法罗斯{RUBY_E#}的时间不多了…很多人都悄悄地这么说。
en: Destiny hasn't got much time left for {RUBY_B#the Old World}Amphoreus{RUBY_E#}... That's what many whisper in the shadows.

ch: 当然，如果刻薄一点…整个{RUBY_B#世界}翁法罗斯{RUBY_E#}恐怕也没有几个人算是真正地活着。只是…黄金裔们还在这里。所以我不会这么去说。
en: To be a bit more strict... there might be only a handful truly alive in {RUBY_B#the entire world}Amphoreus{RUBY_E#}. However... the Chrysos Heirs are here, so I'll hold my tongue on that.

ch: 或者这么说吧——翁法罗斯或许真的在被一些我们尚不清楚的秘密支配…但了解那些秘密的人肯定不在元老院里。
en: Or maybe, let's put it this way: Amphoreus might actually be under the control of enigmatic forces beyond our grasp... But those who hold such knowledge certainly aren't seated in the Council of Elders.

ch: …你好。欢迎来到全世界最大的浴场的图书室。理论上你可以从这里借阅到任何一本在奥赫玛出版的书籍——只要不介意填写调书单之后等待几天。
en: ...Greetings. Welcome to the library of the largest bath in the world. Theoretically, you can access any book published in Okhema, as long as you don't mind waiting a few days after filling out a book-transfer form.

ch: 你可能也知道，天文学在奥赫玛属于禁忌，只有树庭的少数学者可以秘密研究。很不幸，最近逃难来的人实在有点多，他们那里也是僧多粥少，只好把我推荐来浴场工作。
en: As you might be aware, astronomy research is prohibited in Okhema, and only a few scholars in the Grove secretly manage to sneak a peek. With a flood of recent refugees, they don't have enough work for specialists like me and had to recommend me to work here in this bath.

ch: 只是在奥赫玛，天文学…你懂的。虽说《艾格勒曲线论》本身是纯数学的著作，但顶着这个名号，恐怕没什么人愿意和它扯上关系，更不要说资助我出版了。
en: It's just that... astronomy in Okhema... you know how it is. While On Aquila Curves is a book in pure mathematics, its title might deter people from exploring it, let alone securing a sponsor for publication.

ch: 聊聊来奥赫玛之后的研究。
en: Tell me more about your research after arriving in Okhema.

ch: 聊聊树庭。
en: Tell me more about the Grove.

ch: 老身名为塔瓦纳安娜，来自遥远东方的哈图西里城邦。虽说在黑潮之前，我的家乡也曾像花园一样美丽，但赞美{RUBY_B#大地之泰坦}磐岩之脊{RUBY_E#}——果然还是{RUBY_B#奥赫玛}刻法勒庇护下的圣城{RUBY_E#}举世无双。
en: My name is Tavananna, hailing all the way from Hattusili in the far east. While my homeland was a paradise before the black tide — praise the {RUBY_B#Earth Titan}Pillar of Stone{RUBY_E#} — its charm would pale in comparison to the splendor of {RUBY_B#Okhema}the holy city under Kephale's wings{RUBY_E#}.

ch: 打听奥赫玛的事
en: Ask about Okhema.

ch: 「欢迎来到刻法勒庇护的奥赫玛，远道的异乡人。这里是翁法罗斯的最后乐土，也是由黄金裔守护的永恒之城。」
en: "Outlanders from distant lands, welcome to Okhema, the sanctum under the watchful eye of Kephale. This is the Eternal City, shielded by the vigilant Chrysos Heirs, the last paradise in Amphoreus."

ch: 看，只要稍稍关注几个{RUBY_B#公众号}云上广场{RUBY_E#}，我们外地人也能对{RUBY_B#奥赫玛}刻法勒的圣城{RUBY_E#}了如指掌。
en: See? Just follow a few {RUBY_B#public accounts}Nebula Plazas{RUBY_E#}, and even us outlanders can get the lowdown on {RUBY_B#Okhema}Kephale's holy city{RUBY_E#}.

ch: 啊，外面的事吗？其实老身也知道，这年头除了{RUBY_B#奥赫玛}刻法勒的圣城{RUBY_E#}，四下里的确是乱得很。那个黑潮更了不得，哗地一下就淹过去，除了随身细软，那是什么也带不出来。
en: The outside world? Well, I know it's pretty chaotic out there everywhere except in {RUBY_B#Okhema}Kephale's holy city{RUBY_E#}, and that black tide is a real menace. In the blink of an eye, it can swallow a whole city, and you'll barely have enough time to run for your life.

ch: 不过你大可放心！只要来了{RUBY_B#奥赫玛}刻法勒的圣城{RUBY_E#}，有困难找元老院和黄金裔就好。你唯一的烦恼，只可能是无事可做，耐不住寂寞…哈哈。
en: But everything's safe in {RUBY_B#Okhema}Kephale's holy city{RUBY_E#}! If you ever run into trouble, just head to the Council of Elders or find the Chrysos Heirs. Your only trouble might be a bit of boredom... haha.

ch: 「最初的人们聚集在刻法勒脚下，文明的种子便在翁法罗斯萌芽。请看——」
en: "At Kephale's feet, humanity's dawn unfurled. Civilization's roots in Amphoreus twirled."

ch: 悬锋陷落，灾殃骤起，与英雄共赴——
en: Amidst rising plague and crumbling Kremnos. The outlander joins heroes, dispelling great woes.

ch: 但…我相信这只是暂时的。奥赫玛的居民一直都很坚强，未来也会是如此——愿刻法勒庇护我们。
en: But... I believe that this is temporary. Okhema's people have always been hardy, and so will the future — may Kephale protect us.

ch: 奥赫玛敞亮的天光里，随处可见你身影
en: Your form is seen everywhere within the divine light of Okhema

ch: 处理奥赫玛大小事务、为逐火之旅奔波，或许确实是疲累的生活，但于我而言，不过是重复了无数光阴的日常罢了。
en: Dealing with the affairs of Okhema and being constantly on the move for the Flame-Chase Journey could perhaps be considered a tiring life. To me, however, it is no more than a daily routine that I have repeated for ages.

ch: 而那些瑕疵品总会莫名流入奥赫玛的服装市场，标注着我的名字被高价拍卖，成为某位王公贵族的收藏。我虽不乐得见此，但也无权禁止，就随他们去吧。
en: These rejected items always inexplicably end up flowing into Okhema's clothing market. They're tagged with my name and auctioned for high prices to be added to some aristocrat's collection. Though I do not like this, I cannot forbid it. They can do as they please.

ch: 纷争世以来的漫长岁月，有无数世人、无数眷属、无数黄金裔与我、与奥赫玛、与泰坦们产生了命运的交织。不知你要问哪些人？
en: Over the many long years since the Era Bellica, there have been countless people, Titankins, and Chrysos Heirs who have formed bonds of destiny with myself, Okhema, and the Titans. Which ones are you referring to?

ch: 悬锋人中的悬锋人，万敌！
en: The most typical of the Kremnoans, Mydei!

ch: 悬锋人中的悬锋人…如此评价迈德漠斯倒也合理，他无疑是最英勇的悬锋人，在战场上，谁也不能将其击倒。
en: The most typical of the Kremnoans... A fair evaluation of Mydeimos. He is undoubtedly the most heroic warrior among all Kremnoans. He is unstoppable on the battlefield.

ch: 呵…看来你和奥赫玛的居民们一样，都很喜欢吾师。她们确实是黄金裔中最受奥赫玛平民欢迎者，平日里热衷于与街坊打交道，帮人们解决生活中的疑难琐事。
en: Hah... I see you are just as fond of my teachers as the citizens of Okhema are. They indeed enjoy enormous popularity among the people of Okhema. They spend their days deeply committed to communicating with their neighbors and helping people with both great troubles and trivial affairs.

ch: 看来，学习施展「百界门」对于小灰来说还是过于勉强了。不过不要难过，小灰很厉害！不需要「百界门」，也一定能成为翁法罗斯的大英雄！
en: It seems that learning to use the "Century Gate" is a bit too difficult for you now, Gray Hair. But don't be disheartened. You are already amazing! We know that you will become a great hero of Amphoreus even without the power of the "Century Gate"!

ch: 奥赫玛最棒小信使！
en: A round of applause for Okhema's greatest messengers!

ch: 哼哼哼~缇安今天心情也很好！准备去把奥赫玛再飞个遍！
en: Hum-hum-hum! Trianne is in a great mood today as well! Time for a flight over all Okhema again!

ch: 纷争泰坦尚未失神的辉煌年代，只有在其威压之下仍能稳持长枪、浴血而立的王储，才有资格继任悬锋之王。
en: In the glorious era before the Strife Titan fell, only the crown prince who could hold their spear firm under the divine pressure and stand tall under the rain of blood was worthy to become the King of Kremnos.

ch: 我能理解奥赫玛人对尼卡多利的嫌怨，但我也希望你明白，尼卡多利给予翁法罗斯的不止痛苦与恐惧。
en: I can understand the Okhemans' grudge against Nikador. However, I do hope that you will understand that Nikador has gifted more than just suffering and fear to Amphoreus.

ch: 在天谴之矛陷入疯溃前，它也曾是翁法罗斯的守护者。纷争的力量，亦可平息纷争。
en: Before the Lance of Fury fell into madness, they served as the guardian of Amphoreus. The power of Strife is also the queller of strife.

ch: 不知你是否听闻，我曾与自己的故乡有些过节。出于某些原因，我选择了离开悬锋城，在翁法罗斯各地流亡，悬锋孤军则是愿追随我同行的一众乡友。
en: I'm not sure if you have heard this before, but I had a few issues with my hometown. I elected to leave Castrum Kremnos for various reasons and wander across Amphoreus. The Kremnoan Detachment was a group of my fellow countrymen who were willing to follow me.

ch: 后来机缘巧合下，我们与奥赫玛结为联盟，为「金织」主持的逐火之旅效力。期间，悬锋孤军战功赫赫，并在征途中杀回王城，令先王败而授首。
en: Later, we happened to ally ourselves with Okhema and offered our services to the Flame-Chase Journey led by the Goldweaver. During that time, this lone army of Kremnos achieved great military success. Along the expedition, we fought back into their home city and defeated the previous king.

ch: 我自幼离乡，虽流淌着一脉相承的悬锋之血，但对那座王都没有那么眷恋。
en: I left my hometown at a young age. Though I carry the blood of Castrum Kremnos, I bear no affection for that kingdom.

ch: 至于我的同胞们…悬锋人思乡的方式，就是在战场上恣意拼杀。当他们在战场上染血高呼着「宁战死，毋荣归」的时候，我就知道…奥赫玛，无法成为他们真正的家。
en: As for my kin... the Kremnoans show how much they miss their home by fighting without restraint. When I see them on the battlefield, blood-stained and raising a cry of "valorous death before glorious return," I knew... that Okhema could never be their true home.

ch: 哼，与尼卡多利的决战都不足为惧，区区试炼有何可怕？但阻止我手握火种的并非泰坦的试炼…而是悬锋城的试炼。
en: Hmph. If a battle to the death with Nikador could not incite fear in my heart, what could scare me about a mere trial? The thing that stops me from grasping the Coreflame is not the trial of the Titan... but the trial of Castrum Kremnos.

ch: 你是从哪儿找到这块石板的？悬锋城么？
en: Where did you find this slate? Castrum Kremnos?

ch: 要是感兴趣，不如去悬锋城转转，说不定能在记忆的尘堆里翻出些有趣的旧史呢。
en: If you're interested, why not take a look around Castrum Kremnos for yourself? Maybe you will be able to dig up some interesting stories from among the dust of memories.

ch: 你还想追问些什么，但万敌别过脸去，对这个话题缄口不言。
en: Though you have more questions you want to ask, Mydei turns away and remains silent on the topic.

ch: 至于扫墓…奥赫玛的墓园里，有许多长眠者，都是由我来「入殓」的。我会定期前去清扫他们的坟墓——不知为何，我总担忧他们未能安眠。
en: As for visiting graves... Many of the deceased around the graveyards of Okhema were personally embalmed by me. I periodically go to their tombs to dust... I'm not sure why but I'm always concerned that somehow they cannot rest in peace.

ch: 奥赫玛的居民都挺怕你的。
en: The people of Okhema seem to fear you.

ch: 想不到阁下也对翁法罗斯的历史神话故事感兴趣呀！正巧，漫长岁月里我攒了不少有趣的故事，不知阁下想听什么样的？
en: I never would have thought that you were also interested in the mythological tales of Amphoreus, {F#miss}{M#mister}! As luck would have it, I have collected many interesting stories over my long years. What kind of tale would you like to hear?

ch: 如今，浴场精灵们所讲述的翁法罗斯创世神话，或许是目前最符合阁下想要的「正史」了。
en: Perhaps the story that best fits the description of "a formal historical account" is the myth of Amphoreus' creation that the bath sprites have been telling.

ch: 我的故乡啊…那是离奥赫玛最近的度假胜地，气候极为舒适…我家后院有座超级大的花园，我妻子在里面种满了她最爱的薄暮玫瑰。
en: My home... It is the closest scenic holiday spot to Okhema. The climate there is wonderful... My home had a gigantic garden in the rear courtyard. My wife filled it with her favorite dusk roses.

ch: …抱歉，我的措辞有误，那里曾是离奥赫玛最近的度假胜地，嗯…*曾是*。
en: ...Sorry, I should rephrase that. It was the closest scenic holiday spot to Okhema... Was.

ch: 有时候，我会想象我的战友们现在在哪里。也许他们在欧洛尼斯的怀抱中沉眠？也许他们变成了星星，永远守护着翁法罗斯？
en: Sometimes I imagine where my comrades are now. Perhaps they are sleeping under the protection of Oronyx? Perhaps they have become stars to watch over Amphoreus for the rest of time?

ch: 奥赫玛的繁荣离不开每一位居民的建设！啊，新面孔！
en: It is thanks to the hard work of its citizens that Okhema has come to be so prosperous! Ah, a new face!

ch: 奥赫玛真繁荣啊。
en: Okhema truly is prosperous.

ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}刚来奥赫玛啊？欢迎来到我们伟大的城邦！有什么不懂的你可以问我，或者问那些卫兵，知无不言！
en: Are you new to Okhema, {F#girl}{M#boy}? Welcome to our great city! If you need any help, you can always ask me or the guards. We shall help in any way we can!

ch: 能介绍下奥赫玛的情况吗？
en: Can you tell me a bit about Okhema?

ch: 你想知道奥赫玛的什么？让我想想…就从日常生活说起？
en: What would you like to know about Okhema? Let me think... Perhaps I should start from daily life?

ch: 从总体来说，奥赫玛是一个充满机遇的城市。在这里，每个人都有自己的生活之道。
en: Generally speaking, Okhema is a city that is bursting with opportunity. Here, everyone has their own ways of life.

ch: 但在这其中，有一个小秘密——在奥赫玛，真正的影响力往往来自于你认识谁，而不是你是谁。我听说有一位裁缝，她只给「特别顾客」做衣服，从而建立了一个令人惊叹的人际网。
en: However, there is a small secret that lies within all this... In Okhema, true influence comes from who you know, not who you are. I hear that there is a tailor who only makes clothes for "VIP clients." With this, she created an unbelievable personal network.

ch: 如果你能在复杂的人际关系中有一些独特的技巧，或许你就能改变自己的生活——又或者，你能改变的不仅仅是你个人的生活。而这，正是奥赫玛的魅力所在。
en: If you have a unique skill for navigating complex social relationships, there is a chance that you could transform your entire life... Perhaps you will transform even more than just your own life, too. This is where the true charm of Okhema lies.

ch: 在奥赫玛，我们相信欢宴不仅仅是享乐，更是一种社交和交流的方式。通过共饮，人们放下戒备，敞开心扉。这种氛围下，往往能产生最富创造力的想法。
en: In Okhema, we believe that feasts and parties are not simply opportunities to enjoy a good time. We believe that they are also an important method of socializing and communication. It is through drinking together that people drop their guards and open their hearts. It is this kind of atmosphere that is the richest with creative ideas.

ch: 听闻人越多，佳酿的口味就越醇厚。所以…在奥赫玛，没有人会独自痛饮，我们总是习惯了一起面对困难，一起庆祝胜利。
en: I hear that the flavor of this fine wine grows richer the more people there are. So... no one drinks alone in Okhema. We prefer to face life's difficulties together, just as we celebrate our victories together.

ch: 听上去很荒谬，是吧？奥赫玛最著名的演说家，居然害怕面对观众。我每次站在台上，都感觉像是面对一群饥饿的野兽。我的手心冒汗，喉咙发干，脑子一片空白。
en: It sounds absurd, right? The most famous orator in Okhema is scared to speak in front of people. Every time I stand on that stage, I feel like I am facing a pack of hungry beasts. My palms start to sweat, my throat goes dry, and my brain goes blank.

ch: 咳咳咳——各位，听我说，我们奥赫玛的繁荣离不开每一位居民的建设！每一位新生力量都至关重要，所以，我们得拿出来热情来欢迎我们的新朋友，加入奥赫玛！
en: Ahem — Everyone, hear my word! It is thanks to the hard work of its citizens that Okhema has come to be so prosperous! Every new strength that comes to be is crucial to our success. This is why we must show enthusiasm as we welcome our new friends to Okhema!

ch: 要我说那些外邦人就是不地道，闲得没事非要搞什么「分期付款」！从预售到定金截止三个月，等个尾款小半年，就算结了尾款想收到货还得再两个月！
en: If you ask me, these outlanders just don't know how Okhema works! They sit around doing nothing and demand "payment in installments"! They give you three months after the presale to pay the deposit and by the time you make the final payment, six months have slipped by. Even then, it'll still be two months before you even get what you asked for!

ch: 哪能啊，这么说吧——你在奥赫玛的这段时间运气非常不错，就算遇到些波折，最终也都会化险为夷。
en: No way, how about this — you've been very lucky during your time here in Okhema. Even if you encounter a few setbacks, you'll ultimately overcome them in the end.

ch: 那个，我是说，咱也是初来乍到，过去就是个家里蹲，在这奥赫玛实在找不到适合赚钱的营生，所以才从他们的传信石版里学了这么一套……
en: So, what I mean is I only just got here, too. I used to be a real homebody and couldn't find a way to earn a crust in Okhema, so I learned this trick from their teleslate...

ch: …都是些乡间戏言罢了，在这圣城里只能当笑话来讲。而且，如您所见，凡事到了奥赫玛都会变得复杂起来——
en: ...But that's all just rural superstition. Consider it all one big joke while we're here inside the holy city. Plus, as you can see, everything always gets a little more complicated in Okhema...

ch: 奥赫玛人一般会把肥皂的诞生归功于{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}，说是它有次不小心把橄榄油泼洒到了熄灭的火堆上，结果神的祝福因此变化出新的形态，于是就有了肥皂。
en: Okheman folks normally attribute the origins of soap to {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#}, saying the god once accidentally spilled olive oil onto an extinguished bonfire. The god's blessing then took on a new form, and thus soap was invented.

ch: 当然，您要是不在乎融化的肥皂是不是能像蜂蜜一样丝滑，那确实用矿物油都没问题。说到底也就是我们家乡的一种情怀啦！奥赫玛的人就不怎么在乎。
en: Of course, if you don't care whether your soap is as smooth as honey or not, then you can try using a mineral oil. After all, that's the vibe of our hometown! People in Okhema don't really care about too much.

ch: 哦。烟火气也是分很多种的，奥赫玛的烟火气又尤其与众不同。
en: Mmm. There's a variety of warmth to life, and Okhema's is particularly unique.

ch: 所以，等他好了之后，我就劝他、也劝了很多街坊，跟着我们家搬到奥赫玛来——毕竟这里是世界上最安全的城邦，几乎受不到黑潮的威胁。只是……
en: And so once he got better, I convinced him and all the other neighbors to all pick up sticks and move with us to Okhema... After all, this is the safest city in the world, with almost no threat from the black tide. But it's just...

ch: 没什么。黑潮真正降临的时候…一切就是会发生得很突然。可能你准备什么都无济于事。一整个移民旅团，因为半道遭遇了袭击，到头来只有少数几家成功抵达了奥赫玛，拿到了难民身份。
en: It's nothing. When the black tide really comes... it'll all happen very quickly. Maybe nothing we could do would be of any use. Out of an entire caravan of migrants, only a few small families reached Okhema and obtained refugee status due to running into attacks along the way.

ch: 哦，这个也算是奥赫玛独特的景致了。
en: Oh, this is another unique worldview native to Okhema.

ch: 是的，我刚来奥赫玛不久。
en: Yeah, I just came to Okhema not long ago.

ch: 哦，对了，还没来得及自我介绍呢。我叫盖亚，是树庭的学者，目前正在研究水培植物的相关课题。
en: Oh yeah, I haven't had a chance to introduce myself yet. I'm Gaia and I study at the Grove. My research topic is currently looking at hydroponic plants.

ch: 泪葱就是泪葱啊，奥赫玛种了几千年的调味料…你家炒菜不放葱吗？
en: Tear onions are just tear onions. They're a herb that's been used in Okhema for thousands of years... You don't eat onions where you come from?

ch: 植物们可不会说话，它们倾向于把自己的悲痛藏在根茎里，直到我们发现作物减产才窥得一二，届时奥赫玛恐怕也……
en: Plants can't talk, but they tend to hide their grief down inside their roots. We wouldn't notice if anything was going on until there was a drop in yields, by which time I'm afraid Okhema would...

ch: 嘈杂转为了寂静，随后转为了——悲鸣？你只能听见几句含糊不清的，听起来像是「悬锋的清洁工」之类的叫唤。
en: The rabble of noise turns to silence before turning into... wails? You only manage to overhear a few muddled words... Are they saying "Kremnos Cleaner"...?

ch: 啊啊，那些闹事的客人来自悬锋城，他们有人认得我…算是给了我这个老乡一个面子吧。
en: Ahh, those troublesome customers were from Castrum Kremnos. Some of them recognized me... Let's just say they showed their fellow countryman some respect.

ch: 抱歉，我也言重了。不必担心，这里是奥赫玛，于刻法勒的庇护下，即使我全盘托出，我们的生活也不会发生任何改变。
en: My apologies. That sounded threatening. There is no need to worry. This is Okhema, after all. Under the protection of Kephale, even if I tell you everything, our lives will remain unchanged.

ch: 我的确是一位「侍者」，过去也同然。但在悬锋城那时，我的雇主更愿意称我为「清洁工」，或是「解决方案」。
en: I was an "attendant" in the past, too. However, back in Castrum Kremnos, my employers preferred to refer to me as a "cleaner" or a "problem-solver."

ch: 不…悬锋城毁后，我辗转各地，却仅有{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的圣城愿意接纳一位蒙尘的人。那时我就下定决心，不到必要的时候，不再拿起清扫工具。
en: No... After Castrum Kremnos was destroyed, I roamed various places, but only the holy city of {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} would take in a stained fugitive like myself. I made a vow to myself at that moment that I would never again take up my cleaning equipment unless there was an absolute need.

ch: 你好呀！是从外邦来的客人吗？我代表奥赫玛大浴场欢迎你！
en: Hello! Are you a guest from another land? Allow me to welcome you on behalf of the baths of Okhema!

ch: 痛并快乐着——祝你能在奥赫玛玩得开心！
en: You who feel the pain in happiness... May you enjoy your time in Okhema!

ch: 真希望也有这样的命运能眷顾一个只知道躺着的懒人…祝你能在奥赫玛玩得开心！
en: I pray that such a fate could care for an idle person who spends all their days lying down... May you enjoy your time in Okhema!

ch: 哈哈哈哈，奥赫玛的哲学家实在有点多，要是人人都拦着你要辩论的话，那是挺累的！
en: Hahaha, Okhema is rife with philosophers. I'm sure it can become tiresome if everyone is stopping you to discuss such things!

ch: 不过没事儿，我也是一个只知道躺着的懒人——祝你能在奥赫玛玩得开心！
en: Fear not though, I am merely a lazy person who lives only to lie down... May you enjoy your time in Okhema!

ch: 我？我没有什么故事啦，只是一个无所事事的奥赫玛本地人而已。
en: Me? I have no stories. I'm just an Okhema native with too much time on my hands.

ch: 悬锋城人都这么不讲武德吗…
en: Do the people of Castrum Kremnos have no morals in war...?

ch: 悬锋城？不不，其实只有我而已，要不我怎么叫「狡猾的」尤利克赛斯呢~
en: Castrum Kremnos? No, no, this is just me. Why else do you think they call me "Ulixes the Cunning"?

ch: 问得好。很多悬锋战士踏上战场，就是为了迎接荣耀之死，所以他们都不怎么注重防护…这是习俗，没办法。
en: A fine question. Many Kremnos Warriors go to the battlefield in order to claim an honorable death. So they do not pay too much attention to defense... This is a custom, and there is nothing that can be done.

ch: 悬锋军视「在战场上战死」为最高荣耀，「凯旋」次之。「宁战死，毋荣归」是他们最喜欢的口号，而在床榻上了却一生则是最为人所不齿的结局。
en: The soldiers of Kremnos see "death in battle" as the greatest honor. "Triumphant return" is a close second. They like to say: "valorous death before glorious return," believing that peacefully passing away on your deathbed is the most contemptible ending.

ch: 悬锋城战士虽多，却也不是每个人都能成为战士。在踏上这条路之前，你得先做好觉悟。
en: There are many warriors in Castrum Kremnos, but not everyone can become one. One must be properly prepared before embarking on this path.

ch: …不，感觉还是缺乏气势，得想个更帅气的出场词才行。身为克拉特鲁斯大人亲手提拔的悬锋伙友，可不能丢份啊。
en: ...No, still lacks some panache. Got to have a more dashing opener. As a stalwart Kremnos vanguard handpicked by Lord Krateros, I mustn't lose face.

ch: 对，终有一天，我会在战场上对抗悬锋城之敌。届时我必须说出一段令人印象深刻的出场词，不然就会让伙友的身份蒙羞……
en: Yes. One day, I will take to the field and battle Castrum Kremnos' enemies. When the day comes, my opening lines must leave the deepest impression, or I will disgrace the vanguards...

ch: 听说欧利庞王当年御驾亲征，在战场上杀死了足足一百个敌人！以我悬锋伙友的身份，至少也得打二十个才行。
en: I heard that when King Eurypon personally fought in battle, he slew a hundred enemies on the field! As a Kremnos vanguard, I have to at least take out twenty.

ch: 悬锋伙友？克拉特鲁斯？
en: Kremnos vanguard? Krateros?

ch: 悬锋伙友是悬锋城最精锐的士兵。我们拥有整座城邦最好的武器和最严整的军纪，直接对欧利庞王负责，其他士兵也以我们为榜样。
en: The Kremnos vanguard are the most elite soldiers in Castrum Kremnos. We possess the finest weapons and most stringent military discipline in all the city, and are led directly by King Eurypon. Other soldiers hold us in the highest regard.

ch: 像他这样的精锐总共只有五名。他们是悬锋伙友中的佼佼者，也是王最亲密的战友。当王不在时，他们就是军中位阶最高的将领。
en: He is one of only five who hold such elite status. They are exemplars among the Kremnos vanguards, and most bosom comrades of the King. When the king is not around, they wield the utmost authority among the ranks.

ch: 战士，最重要的当然是荣耀。悬锋城有句古话：「身可死，荣耀不可弃。」而证明荣耀的最好方式，就是战胜强敌。
en: Glory is of course the most essential for warriors. Castrum Kremnos has an ancient saying: "The body may die, but glory cannot be abandoned." What better way to prove your honor than to topple a formidable foe?

ch: 只要能杀死一头那样的怪物，你就算是合格的悬锋战士了，但难点在于如何找到活着的钢爪兽…它们都快被杀光了。
en: If you can slay such a monster, you'd be more than qualified to be a Kremnos Warrior. Alas, the problem lies in finding a live Steelclaw Beast... They've almost all been killed off.

ch: 在战场上，懂得协作的战士远比孤军奋战的莽夫有用。回去吧，你不配在悬锋的光辉下献身。
en: On the battlefield, warriors who can work together are far more useful than solitary brutes. Go back. You are not worthy of sacrificing yourself under the radiance of Kremnos.

ch: 在你的眼中，他打造武器的过程朴实无华，除了力气大点、声音响些，似与翁法罗斯之外最平凡的铁匠并无二致。
en: In your eyes, his weapon forging process is unsophisticated, unpretentious. Apart from exhibiting more strength and producing louder clanging, he doesn't seem too different from the most ordinary blacksmiths in Amphoreus.

ch: 在翁法罗斯怎么挑选好武器？
en: How do I choose a good weapon in Amphoreus?

ch: 万敌，也形容过——「血浸多了。」
en: "Drenched in too much blood," said too Mydei.

ch: 万敌？
en: Mydei?

ch: 万敌，许多回，武器坏了。幸存的武器，我修复时，听得见沉吟。
en: His weapon, many times, Mydei has broken. A hum, I hear, when fixing the surviving weapons.

ch: 悬锋人的武器，大多如此。万敌，不同。
en: Mostly like that, Kremnoan weapons are. Different, Mydei's is.

ch: 如今在奥赫玛的山之民，我的族群，曾被使役，悬锋人。
en: Now Mountain Dwellers in Okhema, my people are. Used by Kremnoans, they once were.

ch: 信仰法吉娜的城邦，我们很少去。迄今为止，奥赫玛，我们最喜欢。
en: The city of Phagousa's faith, we rarely visit. Okhema, our favorite, it is so far.

ch: 啊哈，您一定是来自遥远的异邦的贵客，在这奥赫玛，谁人不知老希这「希吉拉与他的翻飞之手铺子」名头啊！
en: Aha, you must be a guest from afar. Here in Okhema, everyone knows of my famed shop, Hygilos and his Whimsy Hand!

ch: 一位生命花园的园丁被你的「球棒动力学理论」深深折服，盛情邀请你加入奥赫玛权威的学术组织。可你深知一切仅是享乐女神的愚弄，你的智慧会在系统时午夜<unbreak>12</unbreak>点后消失……
en: A Garden of Life Gardener is deeply engrossed in your "Baseball Bat Dynamics Theory," and heartily invites you to join a prestigious academic organization in Okhema. But you are deeply aware that this is but a trick by the goddess of pleasure — your intellect will fade when the system time strikes midnight...

ch: 他为何在此，你说不清。他邀请你骑上宽厚的背，你们游遍了奥赫玛的每个角落，你们笑语欢声，度过了难忘的一天……
en: Why he is here, you cannot say. He invites you to ride atop his broad back, and you journey to every corner of Okhema, spending an unforgettable day peppered with laughter...

ch: …一突然窜出的男子打破了你的美梦。他介绍此处为奥赫玛国家一级景区，自己是背你爬上来的导游，需要你交付<unbreak>5000</unbreak>利衡币的门票钱和<unbreak>20000</unbreak>利衡币的辛苦费。
en: ...A man suddenly pops up, disrupting your reverie. He introduces this place as Okhema's top national tourist attraction, and that he was the guide who carried you up the mountain. He also requests that you pay <unbreak>5,000</unbreak> Balance Coins in admission fee and <unbreak>20,000</unbreak> Balance Coins for the "service rendered."

ch: 一段从未听闻的跫音…今日竟果真有幸相逢。你好，奥赫玛的贵客。
en: Sounds of footfalls never before heard... I didn't think I'd actually get to encounter you today. Greetings, honored guest of Okhema.

ch: 我来到奥赫玛也有些时日了。如今我不再受传谕职责所囿，可以用预言替居民们解决生活上的小麻烦。
en: I have arrived in Okhema for some time now. I am not bound by the duty to deliver prophecies any longer, so I use them to help citizens resolve what little troubles they encounter in daily life.

ch: 我曾听说过雅努萨波利斯鼎盛期的辉煌往事。作为「奥赫玛同盟」的重要议事场所，雅努萨波利斯曾是一座富裕安宁的城邦。
en: I once heard of Janusopolis' glorious past during her height of power. As an essential Council venue within the Okhema Alliance, Janusopolis was once a prosperous and peaceful city.

ch: 这天外之音来自欧洛尼斯映照出的可怖景色，纷争掀翻大地，天帷遮蔽天空，一切沦于死亡。这命运一次又一次在翁法罗斯重演，受命运守护的城邦也难幸免……
en: This celestial echo comes from the horrific scene reflected by Oronyx. Strife split the earth, the veil blanketed the sky, and all floundered in death. This fate has been played out in Amphoreus time and time again — even the city protected by destiny itself cannot escape misfortune...

ch: 你好，「异乡人」阁下。上次那张悬锋城的照片，有帮上你们的忙吗？
en: Hello, esteemed outlander. Was that photo of Castrum Kremnos helpful to you?

ch: 这说明，传统的计时方式，根本不适合现在的奥赫玛！
en: This indicates that the traditional method of timekeeping is utterly unsuitable for modern Okhema!

ch: 对吧！要是将这个发现公之于众，我们就能改变奥赫玛沿用至今的历法了！
en: Exactly! By sharing this discovery, we could revolutionize the longstanding calendar system in Okhema!

ch: 奥赫玛会沿用传统的历法，将每天划分为五个时段，是因为人们错误地认为「黎明机器」的光芒不会改变，无法依据光照确定时间。
en: Okhema adheres to a traditional calendar, dividing each day into five distinct periods based on the mistaken belief that the light from the Dawn Device remains constant and cannot be used to determine time.

ch: 但奥赫玛不需要新的历法。
en: But Okhema does not need a new calendar.

ch: 我会去向元老院讲述我的猜想，邀请树庭的学者们见证我的发现。我要在集市里做演讲，在浴池里举办讨论会，我要让所有人都知道这件事。
en: I will go to the Council of Elders with my conjectures and invite Grove scholars to witness my findings. I'll give lectures in the marketplace, and hold discussions in the baths. I want everyone to know about this.

ch: 厉害啊。我在奥赫玛呆了这么久，头一次看这位卫士理人。平常我们怎么叫他都没反应的。
en: Incredible. I've spent years in Okhema and this is the first time I've seen this guard acknowledge anyone. Normally, he never responds, no matter how loudly we call.

ch: 奥赫玛的卫士，基本都是山之民。我们，敬业。
en: Most guards, in Okhema, Mountain Dwellers. We dedicated.

ch: 奥赫玛识字的公民都知道，阿卡索斯早已在黑潮中覆灭。你如何从一座不存在的城邦来？
en: As all who can read in Okhema know that Icatus was long since destroyed by the black tide. How could you have come from a city that does not exist?

ch: 是啊，翁法罗斯还有那么多有趣的事物等着你去开拓，为什么非得花时间来思考这些无用的内容呢。你不认为这是个有价值的话题。
en: You're right. Amphoreus has many interesting things awaiting trailblazing. Why must you waste time reflecting on such pointless conversations? This topic is clearly of no value to you.

ch: 是我刚才的表述有误。我曾来自阿卡索斯，那是我的故乡，黑潮来临前，我携带所有财产逃来了奥赫玛。我来自尚未覆灭的阿卡索斯，这点千真万确。
en: I made a mistake in my last remark. I originally came from Icatus, and that is my home. Before the black tide descended, I took all of my worldly belongings and fled to Okhema. I come from an Icatus that has not yet been destroyed. This point is absolutely true.

ch: 认为这样的讨论毫无意义，不过是思维的惰性。但实际上，整个翁法罗斯无不与之相关。
en: To think that such an argument lacks meaning is no more than mental laziness. In fact, this relates to everything we see in Amphoreus.

ch: 我是奥赫玛的希诺，以教授人们论辩的技巧为生。
en: I am Zeno of Okhema. I make a living by instructing people on the art of debate.

ch: 奥赫玛识字的公民都知道，阿卡索斯早已在黑潮中覆灭。你如何从一座不存在的城邦来？
en: As all who can read in Okhema know that Icatus was long since destroyed by the black tide. How could you have come from a city that does not exist?

ch: 是我先前的表述有误。我曾来自阿卡索斯，那是我的故乡，黑潮来临前，我携带所有财产逃来了奥赫玛。我来自尚未覆灭的阿卡索斯，这点千真万确。
en: I made a mistake in my last remark. I originally come from Icatus, and that is my home. Before the black tide descended, I took all of my worldly belongings and fled to Okhema. I come from an Icatus that has not yet been destroyed. This point is absolutely true.

ch: 数学是阐述必然性的知识，哪怕悬锋城的战士全部改信墨涅塔，圆依旧是圆，不会变成方形。但「阿卡索斯」的存在，必须依赖现实历史。
en: Mathematics explains absolute and certain truth. If all the warriors of Castrum Kremnos were to suddenly follow Mnestia, a circle would still be a circle. It would not change shape. However, the existence of "Icatus" absolutely relies on real history.

ch: 要是「阿卡索斯」真实存在，请问「奥赫玛」与「悬锋城」是否也该真实存在？「神悟树庭」与「哀丽秘榭」是否也真实存在？
en: If "Icatus" truly exists, do "Okhema" and "Castrum Kremnos" also truly exist as well? What about "Grove of Epiphany" and "Aedes Elysiae"?

ch: 哈，我就猜到你准会相信他那套言论。要是在奥赫玛公然质疑阿卡索斯的真实性，是要引起众怒的。但没关系，我愿意为我的论点辩护。
en: Hah, I knew that you would favor his argument. If Okhema were to publicly doubt the truth of Icatus' existence, it would be certain to incite outrage. I do not mind, however. I am willing to defend my theory.

ch: 若是有座城邦，远在翁法罗斯的另一头，从未与奥赫玛有过任何通信，按你刚才的论点，那座城邦的人们便全都消失不见了。
en: If there were another city on the other side of Amphoreus that had never had contact with Okhema, then according to your logic, the people of this city would disappear without a trace.

ch: 我正在尝试用不同深浅的灰色来表现天空的光照变化，艾格勒的仁慈赋予了奥赫玛如此美妙的景象。
en: I'm experimenting with various shades of gray to capture the shifting light in the sky. Aquila's mercy has blessed Okhema with such breathtaking scenery.

ch: 比如说，当我画奥赫玛的集市时，我不会去关注水果的颜色，而是专注于集市的喧嚣、人们的欢笑、讨价还价的热闹。
en: Take, for instance, when I paint the marketplace in Okhema, I don't focus on the colors of the fruit. Instead, I capture the buzz of the crowd, their laughter, and the vibrant bartering.

ch: 所以我很注重奥赫玛的历史，在资料收集上花费了大量时间——结果到头来，他们却总说我能看到过去，而不知道我背后有多努力。
en: I place great importance on the history of Okhema, dedicating an enormous amount of time to research. Yet, people often think I can simply see into the past, unaware of the extensive effort I put in behind the scenes.

ch: 怎么样，在奥赫玛的生活还习惯吗？有没有稳定的收入？千里迢迢来到圣城，可别一不小心把自己饿死了！
en: How are you finding life in Okhema? Do you have a stable income? It'd be a real shame if you came all this way to the holy city only to end up starving!

ch: 奥赫玛会饿死人？
en: Do people starve in Okhema?

ch: 或许吧…可你别说，我刚来奥赫玛的时候，因为在黑潮里受了惊吓，也是足足缓了一个星期才敢出门——总是害怕有什么怪物从地里、水里突然就冒出来。
en: Maybe... but honestly, when I first arrived in Okhema, I was so shaken by the black tide that it took me a full week before I even dared to go outside. I kept imagining some creature would suddenly pop out of the ground or water.

ch: 当然，也有很多人觉得奥赫玛本来就没有什么捷足飞贼，或许这个传闻是个谎言吧。
en: A lot of people also believe there never was a Phantom Strider in Okhema. It's all just a tall tale.

ch: 有个人在传信石版上发辩论场说，他在投奔奥赫玛的路上和家人走散了，结果遇到了一个不太会说人话的黑衣人，把刀剑架在他脖子上问他今天是星期几！
en: Someone on the teleslate forum shared that he got separated from his family en route to Okhema and encountered a man in black who was hardly able to talk. He confronted him with a sword, demanding to know the day of the week.

ch: 虽然不能做到像万敌哥哥那样刀枪不入，但我会证明，只要修炼得当，奥赫玛人也能拥有不输给悬锋人的勇武！
en: Although I can't achieve the invincibility of great Mydei, I will prove that with proper training, we Okhemans can possess courage and strength rivaling that of the Kremnoans!

ch: 是啊，我知道悬锋城有不少坏家伙，伤害了很多其他的城邦…但也有很多好人！像万敌哥哥和他的孤军，愿意保护我们……
en: Yeah, I know there are plenty of bad folks in Castrum Kremnos who've caused harm to many other cities... but there are also a lot of good people! Like Mydei and his detachment, who are willing to protect us...

ch: 我猜，万敌是你的偶像？
en: I guess Mydei is your idol?

ch: 那不是当然的吗？无敌又帅气，既是悬锋城的王储，也是奥赫玛的救星……
en: Isn't that obvious? He's invincible and dashing, not only the crown prince of Castrum Kremnos but also the savior of Okhema...

ch: 重要的是，他还愿意教我怎么锻炼！用什么来称赞万敌哥哥都不为过！
en: Most importantly, he's willing to teach me how to train! No amount of praise for Brother Mydei is too much!

ch: 只可惜悬锋孤军只让悬锋人加入…不过没关系，万敌哥哥说了，悬锋城有悬锋城的荣耀，奥赫玛也可以有奥赫玛的荣耀…嘿嘿。
en: It's just a pity that the Kremnoan Detachment only allows the Kremnoans to join... But it's fine. Mydei said that Castrum Kremnos has its own pride, and Okhema can have its own pride too... Hehe.

ch: 据说在悬锋人的故乡悬锋城，城外深不见底的壕沟上悬着几条碗口粗的巨大铁链子——那些挑战体力与勇气的人，就会在那几条铁链子上玩折返跑。
en: It's said that in the homeland of the Kremnoans, Castrum Kremnos, there are massive iron chains, each as wide as a bowl, suspended over the bottomless trenches outside the city. Those who challenge their strength and courage will perform shuttle runs on those chains.

ch: 虽然咱们奥赫玛没有他们说的那种铁链，但确实有不少道路都架设在云端。
en: Although we Okhemans don't have iron chains like that, a lot of our roads are built high in the clouds.

ch: 悬锋孤军…也就是那些住在奥赫玛的悬锋人，就会专门挑这些路线来跑步锻炼。
en: The Kremnoan Detachment, also known as those Kremnoans living in Okhema, specifically choose these routes for their running training.

ch: 咳咳，严格来说，这个在奥赫玛是犯法的！不过因为不会造成任何严重后果，所以就算被发现了应该也只会被口头警告吧……
en: Ahem, strictly speaking, this is illegal in Okhema! But since it doesn't cause any serious harm, even if you're caught, you'll probably just get a verbal warning...

ch: 偷东西的人练习的是潜行与保密的能力，找回东西的人则是练习侦察和寻物的本事。万敌哥哥说无影寻宝风靡之后，悬锋城就再也没有小偷了…至少没有活着的。
en: Those who steal practice their stealth and secrecy, while those retrieving the items hone their scouting and tracking skills. Mydei said that ever since shadowless treasure hunting became popular, there haven't been any thieves in Castrum Kremnos... or at least, none alive.

ch: 哦，这个最接近奥赫玛本身就有的活动！捷足战舞其实就是光脚跳舞…不过动作比较大开大阖，而且是穿戴好盔甲、全副武装地跳。
en: Oh, this is the activity most similar to what Okhema itself has! Swift-footed dancing is actually just barefoot dancing... but with much larger and more sweeping movements, and it's done while fully armored, armed to the teeth.

ch: 嘿嘿，不是我自夸，别看悬锋人会吃苦，真论锻炼效果，应该还是我自己发明的这个「飞瀑潜立」最有潜力！万敌哥哥都夸过咱呢！
en: Hehe, not to brag, but even though the Kremnoans endure a lot of hardship, I'd say the training method I invented, Flying Waterfall Stance, has the most potential! Even Brother Mydei has praised it!

ch: 慌乱的神色、结巴的话语、拙劣的借口，出卖了他的心虚。为了翁法罗斯民众的安危，正义的球棒侠决定问个水落石出。
en: The panicked expression, stammering words, and clumsy excuses betray his guilt. For the safety of the people of Amphoreus, the Galactic Baseballer of justice decides to get to the bottom of it.

ch: 嘴上嚷嚷着翁法罗斯人传承浴场文化义不容辞，我看你们呀，只是想趁机秀出你们的健身成果，享受旁人的垂涎。
en: You keep going on about how the people of Amphoreus are duty-bound to uphold the heritage of bath culture, but I see through you all — you just want to show off your fitness gains and enjoy the envy of others.

ch: 这可不是一时的风尚，也不是奥赫玛的专属，而是翁法罗斯所有城邦的通病，只是不同地方的狂热程度有高有低。
en: This is not just a passing trend, nor is it exclusive to Okhema. It is a common affliction in all the city-states of Amphoreus, though the level of fanaticism varies from place to place.

ch: 我、我哪有羡慕他们！我正在准备树庭的考试，我要成为学者，才不要做这种肌肉发达，头脑空空的人！
en: I—I'm not jealous of them! I'm preparing for the Grove exams. I want to become a scholar, not someone with big muscles and an empty head!

ch: 「塔拉萨波利斯」，对吧？是个我从来没听说过的名字呢，原来翁法罗斯还有这样一个城邦吗？
en: Thalassopolis, right? I haven't heard of that name before. So, there's a city like that in Amphoreus?

ch: 怀着微妙的心情，你给这人简单讲了讲自己与某位「寒腿叔叔」的孽缘。当然，是翁法罗斯本地化版本。
en: With certain strange emotions, you briefly explained your relationship with a certain Mr. Cold Feet. Of course, you gave them the localized Amphoreus version.

ch: 放心吧，只要您予我信心便足矣，我会用自己的力量向奥赫玛证明。
en: Rest assured. As long as you grant me confidence, that is enough. I will use my own strength to prove myself to Okhema.

ch: 放心吧，只要您予我信心便足矣，我会用自己的力量向奥赫玛证明。
en: Rest assured. As long as you grant me confidence, that is enough. I will use my own strength to prove myself to Okhema.

ch: 不可能，我就是黄金裔！况且，自我决心踏上这条道路，我便无所畏惧，就像神话中真正的英雄一样。我一定会拯救奥赫玛的诸位……
en: No way. I am a Chrysos Heir! Plus, I have decided to take this path for myself. So, I have no fear! Just like those heroes in the myths! I will save everyone in Okhema...

ch: 嗯…看你的打扮是来自外乡，一定还没适应这边的环境吧？这本或许能让你对奥赫玛的印象更加轻松些。这是拓本，无需归还，拿着慢慢读吧。
en: Well... judging by your attire, you must be from out of town and haven't quite adjusted to the environment here yet? This book might make your impression of Okhema a bit more lighthearted. It's a rubbing, so you don't need to return it. Take it and read at your own pace.

ch: 我猜你是想问，为什么会在云石天宫里建个图书馆，是吧？嗯…这要扯到几十个光历年前——记得刚好是第四十三个奥赫玛百年祭那会。
en: I guess you're wondering why there's a repository in the Marmoreal Palace, right? Well... this goes back several Light Calendar years ago — exactly during the <unbreak>43</unbreak>rd Okhema Centennial Festival.

ch: 也不知是该怪他们特立独行，还是该夸他们懂得生活了。谁让这旁边就杵着全翁法罗斯最好的休憩处呢？
en: I don't know whether I should blame them for being so unconventional, or praise them for knowing how to enjoy life. After all, right next door is the best resting place in all of Amphoreus.

ch: …我原本还想为您再做些更深入的介绍，但忽然想起，即便在奥赫玛，水漫琴也仅剩这一台。多做讲解，也不过是无用的知识罢了。
en: ...I thought to give you a more elaborate introduction, but I suddenly remembered that there is only one such water lyre left, even in Okhema. It's pointless to tell you more about the instrument.

ch: 奥赫玛的人们称呼它为水漫琴，但对我们来说，它就是普通的琴。我原以为这些精密的机械早已被黑潮吞没了，没想到在此处还能见到。
en: The Okhemans call it a water lyre. But to us, it is an ordinary lyre. I thought these intricate devices were long devoured by the black tide. I never thought I'd see one here.

ch: …忧伤么。初到奥赫玛的时候，我确实有些慌张。全知全能的刻法勒背负天空，搭建起的小小庇护所，其中是否有我的容身之处？我不知道。
en: ...Sorrow, was it? I do admit that when I first arrived in Okhema, I was a little anxious. A tiny shelter built beneath the sky that is borne by the omniscient, omnipotent Kephale. Would there be a place for me here? I did not know.

ch: …希冀么。那是自然，若是不抱有看到明日晨光的愿望，又该如何度过这个漫长的夜晚呢？即使奥赫玛不受黑夜侵袭，也不该忘记心怀信念。
en: ...Aspirations, huh. That is only natural. If we do not cling to the hopes of tomorrow's dawn, how will we even endure this long night? Even if Okhema cannot be invaded by darkness, we should not forget to have faith in our hearts.

ch: 奇美拉是奥赫玛的宝物，大家都要爱护它们呀！
en: Chimeras are treasures of Okhema. Everyone should cherish them!

ch: 是吧！可惜成为园丁需要很长时间的培训，不然，我得要让所有奥赫玛的人们都来亲身领略奇美拉的可爱之处！
en: I know, right! A pity it takes a lot of training to become a Gardener, otherwise I'd make sure everyone in Okhema gets a chance to experience how totally adorable chimeras are!

ch: 请随便问吧，整个奥赫玛恐怕都找不到比我更了解它们的人了。
en: Ask away. I don't think there's anyone else in Okhema who knows more about them than me.

ch: 你应该听说过，奇美拉并非原本就生活在翁法罗斯的生物，而是花园的学者，使用「生命树种」培育出来的。
en: You must've heard before that the chimeras aren't actually native to Amphoreus. Rather, they were grown using the Seed of Life by the Garden's scholars.

ch: 要我说，奇美拉是瑟希斯赐给翁法罗斯的礼物——上哪再去找这么可爱又听话的小动物呢。复原古生物只是一厢情愿的想法，我们该接受现实才对。
en: I say, the chimera is a gift that Cerces bestowed upon Amphoreus — where else can you find such a cute and obedient critter? Reviving creatures of the past is nothing but a pipe dream, so we should just accept reality.

ch: 现在，奇美拉和奥赫玛的人们相处得可好啦，大家都很喜欢它们。
en: Now, the chimeras get along so well with the people of Okhema. Everyone loves them.

ch: 严格来说，生命花园是个学术组织，负责研究生命相关的知识。这里的学者很多都在神悟树庭进修过，接受瑟希斯的智慧熏陶。
en: Strictly speaking, the Garden of Life is an academic organization charged with researching all aspects related to life. Most of the scholars here studied at the Grove of Epiphany, where they were influenced by Cerces' wisdom.

ch: 但最近，随着奥赫玛要处理的现实问题越来越多，花园也会帮助居民们解决些实际麻烦，譬如照料大地兽啦，消除农作物的病害啦…之类的。
en: But in recent times, as Okhema has more realistic problems to resolve, the Garden has also become a haven to alleviate her people's worries. They can care for dromases and eliminate diseases from crops here, among other things.

ch: 说起来…我也有段时间没去树庭听讲座了。不知道他们有没有什么新发现……
en: Speaking of which... I haven't listened to a lecture at the Grove for quite a while. I wonder if they've made any new discoveries...

ch: 如果说奥赫玛真是最后的方舟…那么这方舟究竟在用什么形式保护我们的过去，就难免令人焦虑了。
en: If Okhema really becomes the final ark... Then, exactly how this ark is preserving our past is a cause for anxiety.

ch: 来聊聊翁法罗斯的古生物吧。
en: Let's talk about Amphoreus' prehistoric creatures.

ch: 行吧。古生物的知识…对翁法罗斯的未来可能确实没有什么帮助。
en: Fine... Knowledge on prehistoric creatures... probably isn't of much help to Amphoreus' future.

ch: 乐意效劳！那么首先，你了解「神悟树庭」吗？
en: Gladly! First, do you know about the Grove of Epiphany?

ch: 那些花园与庭院后来成为了「神悟树庭」——一个供世人醒神、求识、悟道的学院，一个在学术上兼容并包、百花齐放的圣地。
en: The garden and grove later became the Grove of Epiphany — an academy where people awoke their minds, sought knowledge, and invoked enlightenment. A sanctuary where academia embraces inclusivity and teems with diversity.

ch: 在翁法罗斯，人们通常将那些具有一定规模与影响的学术群体称作「学派」，而我便属于树庭七大学派中的「曳石学派」。
en: In Amphoreus, people usually refer to these structured and influential academic groups as Schools, and I belong to the Helkolithist school, one of seven in the Grove.

ch: 怎么会呢？ 浴池能让大家心平气和地坐在一块，这么好的地方，奥赫玛独此一家！
en: Not really? Everyone's calm and gathered in a bath. It's the most perfect place in all of Okhema!

ch: 简单介绍下，我叫尤利娅，是树庭的学者，现在是浴场的常客。我喜欢在这里跟普罗大众宣讲我一生信奉的瑟希斯之道。
en: An introduction is in order: I am Julia, a Grove scholar, and now a bath regular. I always enjoy the chance to regale everyone here about Cerces, the path to which I have devoted my life.

ch: 啧，我看你样貌不凡，没想到竟是和树庭那群老古板一样的庸人，真是可悲。
en: Tsk, you look like an extraordinary person, but you're just as intransigent as those old farts in the Grove. What a shame.

ch: 你别看树庭号称什么「智慧殿堂」，什么「瑟希斯庇佑的圣地」，其实都是些虚名。
en: The Grove may have monikers like the "Erudite Temple," "Cerces' Blessed Sanctuary" and whatnot, but they're all just lies.

ch: 可你看看奥赫玛，泰坦的奇迹随处可见，凡人有什么资格收费？知识为什么要付费？法吉娜会对泉水收费吗？刻法勒会对光照收费吗？
en: But look at Okhema — the Titans' miracles can be seen throughout her streets. What right do mortals have to charge a fee? Why is knowledge behind a paywall? Would Phagousa charge for their springwater? Would Kephale make you pay to bask in its light?

ch: 所有知识都该对公众开放，你要是想从树庭学点什么，来找我，我会免费教你。让我姐姐那种拿学问谋利的家伙看看，真正的知识是怎么传播的！
en: All knowledge should be shared with the public. If there's anything you wish to learn from the Grove, just approach me, and I'll teach you everything free of charge. I'll show those selfish scholars like my sister how true knowledge should be disseminated!

ch: 你是树庭学者，都研究什么？
en: What are you researching as a Grove scholar?

ch: 我的研究？哦，那可太宏大了！我主要研究泰坦思维与翁法罗斯世界法则的关系。你知道吗，整个奥赫玛，不，整个翁法罗斯都是泰坦思想的具现！
en: My research? Oh, it's so all-encompassing! I mainly study the relationship between Titanic thoughts and the natural laws of Amphoreus. Did you know that the whole of Okhema, nay, the whole of Amphoreus, is a manifestation of the Titanic thoughts?

ch: 比如说，我发现雅努斯的思维模式可能影响了整个世界的空间结构。你有没有注意到奥赫玛的建筑总是成对出现？这可能就是雅努斯双面性的体现！
en: For example, I noticed that Janus' mode of thought may have influenced the entire world's spatial structure. Have you noticed that Okhema's buildings always come in pairs? This could be an allusion to Janus' duality!

ch: 最重要的是，这里是自由的！没有人管我，告诉我该研究什么，不该研究什么。我可以尽情地思考那些看似不着边际，但可能改变整个翁法罗斯的伟大理论！
en: Most importantly, this is the epitome of freedom! No one to stick it to me, tell me what I should be working on or otherwise. I can ruminate on the most far-fetched of concepts that may turn out to be grand theories that can change the whole Amphoreus!

ch: 所以，别以为我是在逃避工作。恰恰相反，我在这里做的研究，比在那个被世俗观念束缚的树庭里有意义多了！
en: So don't think I'm trying to avoid work here. Quite the contrary, I am conducting research that is far more meaningful than whatever is in the Grove, fettered by shackles of worldly sentiments!

ch: 我们姐妹俩的理念就是水火不容。她想把树庭变成一个解决日常问题的作坊，而我则要让它成为彻底开源的源泉，让翁法罗斯的每个人都能感受到瑟希斯的智慧！
en: Our philosophies are as opposite as fire and ice. She wants to turn the Grove into some workshop that can resolve everyday problems, while I want to turn it into a wellspring of openly shared knowledge, for everyone in Amphoreus to revel in Cerces' wisdom!

ch: 对了，差点忘记跟你说——以后你要是想从树庭学点什么，尽管来找我！记住我的话：知识应当是自由且免费的！这才是瑟希斯的精神！
en: Oh yes, almost forgot to tell you — if you want to learn anything from the Grove in the future, just look me up instead! Remember my words: Knowledge should be free of charge! This is what Cerces would want!

ch: 别天真了，树庭这么多学者，吃饭不用钱？研究学问不用钱？没钱怎么做学问？你好歹也体谅下我吧，家里的生活费还是我出的呢。
en: Don't be naive. There are so many scholars in the Grove. Their food and studies all require funds. We won't last without money. I'm the one covering our living expenses. Have a heart!

ch: 就是树庭的一些研究成果，在瑟希斯的祝福下，我们将自己的心血储存在特殊的「种子」上，开放授权给特定的人群阅读。
en: Some research outcomes from the Grove. With Cerces' blessing, we have stored our efforts into special "seeds," and are granting permission to certain groups to read them.

ch: 什么呀，我这是树庭授权的正当生意，每到机缘月还要向元老院交税的。
en: What kind of question is that? This is officially authorized by the Grove, and I pay my taxes to the Council of Elders every Month of Fortune.

ch: 瞧你这一脸迷惑的模样，住在花园附近的居民们倒是见怪不怪了——你一定是新来奥赫玛不久吧？
en: You look bewildered, though it's a common sight to those living near the Garden. You must be new to Okhema?

ch: 自我介绍一下，我叫维普萨尼娅，是神悟树庭的财务官。不过现在嘛...就当我是个树庭知识的推销员吧…嗯，按我妹妹的话来说我就是「到处推销」。
en: Allow me to introduce myself: I am Vipsania, Grove of Epiphany Financial Officer. But now... just think of me as a Grove Knowledge salesperson... Mm, my sister would say I'm "peddling wares."

ch: 既然你刚到奥赫玛不久，你想买点「种子」带走吗？里面都是树庭的珍藏噢。怎么样，要不要来点？
en: Since you haven't been in Okhema for long, how would you like to take home some "seeds"? They're all precious treasures from the Grove. How about it, care for some?

ch: 对了，你知道神悟树庭吧？人们都说，我们那儿有着翁法罗斯数一数二的聪明人。不过你可别在意这些吹捧，归根结底，做学问的基础还是做实验。没钱没平台，再聪明也白搭。
en: Oh yes, you know of the Grove of Epiphany, yes? Everyone says that we house all the brightest minds in Amphoreus. But don't listen to all that horn-tooting. At its heart, experimentation builds the foundation of knowledge. Without money or platform, even the smartest scholar is a fool.

ch: 我可不是那种倒卖树庭资源的混蛋，我这都是定期捞取「沉没树种」，看看适不适合应用到我们生活中，有合适的才好放到市场来叫卖。
en: I'm not one of those scoundrels reselling Grove assets. These are Sunken Tree Seeds that I regularly procure to see if they help with our daily lives. Only successful product tests are sold on the market.

ch: 怎么样，想不想为奥赫玛的知识传播出份力？
en: How about it, do you want to contribute to spreading the knowledge of Okhema?

ch: 糟了，传信石版有点问题，我和树庭那边没联系上。等修好了我再喊你，现在先把你放在预定名单里，抱歉抱歉……
en: Drats, there's something wrong with the teleslate. I didn't manage to link up with the Grove. I'll holler when I fix it, for now I'll put you on the reservation list, so sorry about that...

ch: 不过我得提前跟你打声招呼，可能是之前销量太好，树庭那边的传信石版有点问题，我现在还没联系上我的师门。
en: But I have to let you know in advance — there might be problems with the Grove's teleslate, perhaps due to how good sales were previously. I can't get in touch with my school.

ch: 至于其他的…什么类型的知识种子都有，这个得看我们树庭的学者们最近在研究什么了。
en: As for the others... We've got all kinds of Knowledge Seeds. It all depends on what our Grove scholars have been researching recently.

ch: 种子其实就是我们树庭的一些研究成果，在瑟希斯的祝福下，我们将自己的心血储存在特殊的「知识树」上，攫取其成熟的果实。
en: Seeds are actually some research outcome from the Grove. With Cerces' blessing, we stored our efforts in a special "Knowledge Tree," then harvest its ripened fruit.

ch: 通过特定的操作，可以在传信石版上开放授权给指定的人浏览。至于这背后的原理，除了瑟希斯的赐福之外，和树庭多年来传承的技术也有关系。具体原理太过复杂，我就不跟你赘述了。
en: By employing certain methods, we can authorize a specific individual to browse our teleslate. As for the underlying principles, apart from Cerces' blessing, it's also to do with the techniques passed down by the Grove for many years. The exact details are really complex, so I won't bother you with them.

ch: 至于我，我啊，就是把这些高深莫测的知识整理成大家能看懂的东西，然后再反哺树庭的开支。说白了，就是把知识变成钱，再把钱变成更多知识。
en: As for me, well, I organize this profound knowledge into something that everyone can understand, then support the Grove's expenses with the money. In simpler terms, it's turning knowledge into money, and that money into even more knowledge.

ch: 哎，我们小时候关系很好，长大了就不行了。她和我虽然同样是树庭的一份子，但观念上…只能说分歧很大。
en: *sigh* We were very close as kids, but not after we grew up. We may both be from the Grove, but in terms of perspective... I can only say that we don't see eye to eye.

ch: 真正的悬锋战士就该痛饮鲜血！…不过像我这样的人没什么机会踏上战场，只能用蜜酿来替代了。
en: True Kremnos warriors should slake their thirst in blood! ...But for someone like me who'd never get a chance to step foot on the battlefield, I'll have to make do with this honey brew.

ch: 法吉娜的生性并不残暴。作为替代，它向尼卡多利献上与鲜血颜色相近的石榴汁，借由信徒之手发放给了每一个悬锋城人…嗝，确实好喝。仔细想想，真正的鲜血应该又腥又咸吧……
en: Phagousa's nature isn't so brutal. In replacement, they gave Nikador pomegranate juice which looked a lot like blood, and had their disciples distribute them into the hands of everyone in Castrum Kremnos... *hic* This sure is tasty. When you think about it, actual blood probably tastes all fishy and salty, no...?

ch: 何况对悬锋战士来说，肚量也是战斗力的一部分——英雄不光要能打，还要能喝，不然怎么撑得过庆功宴！
en: And to a Kremnos Warrior, a belly's capacity is also part of battle ability — heroes must be good at both fighting and drinking! How else would we last through the victory banquet!

ch: 趣事？那就不得不提之前对奥赫玛的战争中，悬锋军遭遇断粮的事了。
en: Interesting stories? I must talk about the war on Okhema last time, when the Kremnoan army ran into a food shortage.

ch: 我虽然心里没底，但也不能打击士气，就抱着试试看的想法四处闲逛，没想到阴差阳错地溜到了奥赫玛的营帐附近——可能他们看我不像个战士吧。
en: I wasn't too confident, but I wasn't about to demoralize them. So I wandered around to see if I could think of anything. Who'd thought, I somehow ended up nearby the Okhema camp — I probably didn't look like a soldier to them.

ch: 至于可怜的奥赫玛人，他们的生产水平很高，几头角猪算不上什么损失——我还留了借条呢。
en: As for the poor Okhemans, they've got a rich production line. A few hornswines mean nothing to them. I even left an IOU.

ch: 但你别说，我还真上过战场。先前对奥赫玛发起的特别行动中，欧利庞王征发了所有的士兵…我也只能被迫跟着去了。
en: Though I have deployed before. In that special operation against Okhema, King Eurypon mobilized the entire army... I was forced to tag along.

ch: 至于表现…这么跟你说吧，悬锋城的士兵总共分为三种人：鲁莽者、勇敢者和犹豫者，而我是第四种——怯懦者。
en: As for how I did... I'll just break it down for you — there are three kinds of soldiers in Castrum Kremnos: The brash, the brave, and the balky. And I'm the fourth kind: The craven.

ch: 哎，你有所不知，外界总喜欢传些不着调的谣言，什么我能看到奥赫玛的过去啦，我的眼睛因为受泰坦的赐福而不堪重负啦。
en: Oh, you might not be aware, but there are all sorts of wild rumors out there. People say I can see into Okhema's past, or that my eyes bear the heavy gift of a Titan's blessing.

ch: 奥赫玛总是这么亮，不知道丹恒为什么能睡得那么好。
en: Okhema is always so bright, I can't understand how Dan Heng manages to sleep so soundly.

ch: 希望离开翁法罗斯之后，我也能记得在这里的时光。
en: I hope that I can still hold onto these memories even after I leave Amphoreus.

ch: …我？奥赫玛的住民竟无知到这般地步，连「雅努斯之驹」的名号都未曾听过吗？哼哼，刻法勒号称全知全能，信奉者却如此孤陋寡闻，你真是不称职呐。
en: ...Me? Are the citizens of Okhema so foolish that they've never even heard of Janus' Steed? Hmph, Kephale claims to be all-knowing and all-powerful, yet their followers sure don't know a thing. Incompetence!

ch: 自雅努斯为天地划定界限起，我们便侍奉在左右，打开通道，传达神谕。神驹身负使命，奔走在翁法罗斯的各处，难道你就从来没遇见过？
en: Ever since Janus carved the boundary between heaven and earth, we have been there serving, opening passages, and delivering prophesies. Have you never seen a divine foal cantering about Amphoreus on their missions?

ch: 雅努萨波利斯，不愧是翁法罗斯的中心。能来到这里，老夫的人生也算是圆满了呐。
en: Janusopolis... It's really what you'd expect from the heart of Amphoreus. Being able to make it here, I feel like my life has been worth something.

ch: 哈哈，年轻人你这么说也没错。雅努斯之城虽然崇高，但也是充满人间烟火的一座城市，比起奥赫玛或是哀地里亚来没有本质区别。只不过，对老夫来说……
en: Haha, you're not wrong, young one. Though the city of Janus is majestic, it is also a place where people live their normal daily lives, no different to Okhema or Aidonia. However, in my eyes...

ch: 翁法罗斯可没有哪条河叫做银河，冥河倒是有。怎么，你在冥河里游过？
en: Everyone in Amphoreus knows that one cannot traverse the sea of stars! The River of Souls is always available, though. Have you tried a dip?

ch: 神话里对刻法勒的赞誉，不过是由成功者书写的历史罢了。所谓全知全能，皆因奥赫玛是当今翁法罗斯最鼎盛的城邦。
en: They say that history is written by the winners, and that is precisely what has happened with the constant praise of Kephale in mythology. This so-called all-knowing and all-powerful nature comes from nothing more than the fact that Okhema is the most prospering city in Amphoreus.

ch: 我都想…整理好了，翁法罗斯正史第一章，万径之门开天辟地……
en: I want to... I've already prepared what I would write. Historical account of Amphoreus, chapter one: The Gate of Infinity splits the heavens and the earth...

ch: 退一万步说，即使悬锋人真的对雅努萨波利斯图谋不轨，只要我们以神谕统合众邦，悬锋城又有何惧？
en: Even if the Kremnoans did have a plot against Janusopolis, we need simply unite the nations through our prophecies. What is there to fear about Castrum Kremnos?

ch: 啊…没问题。要聊聊奥赫玛推理协会的故事吗？我的那几位朋友…他们都是很好的人。
en: Ah, sure. Would you like to hear about the Okhema Deduction Society? My friends... they are wonderful people.

ch: 他还是奥赫玛棍球协会的成员，据说打棍球的水平比「曳石学派」的很多人还要好…不过这个也有可能是他自吹自擂的结果。
en: He's also a member of the Okhema Stickball Society, rumored to play better than many from the Helkolithists, though that might be his own brag.

ch: 后来我们几个在浴场偶遇的同好就互相留了联系方式，成立了这个自封为「奥赫玛推理协会」的活动社。
en: Then, since we kept seeing each other at the baths, our group exchanged contacts and established what we now proudly call the "Okhema Deduction Society."

ch: 这工作听上去有点像所谓的「冒险者」，但其实不过每天在传信石版上跟进树庭发布的各种信息，推测黑潮的活动情况，从中规划一条相对安全的寻物路线……
en: While it might sound like the life of an "adventurer," it's mostly about checking on the teleslate information from the Grove, predicting the movements of the black tide, and mapping out a relatively safe route for retrieval missions...

ch: 一起去树庭的时候。
en: When we went to the Grove together.

ch: 的确，相伴旅行能让人发现朋友身上不为人知的一面。即使是相识已久的缇安老师，在这次树庭之行里，同样露出些隐蔽的性情。
en: Indeed, traveling together often reveals sides of a friend that were previously unseen. Even Trianne, whom I've known for so long, showed hidden sides of her personality on this trip to the Grove.

ch: 诶？问我对阁下的初印象吗？说来可笑，当时看到阁下与白厄站在一块儿，我还以为奥赫玛又迎来一位板正的救世主了呢。
en: Oh? What was my first impression of you? Funny story — when I first saw you standing with Phainon, I thought Okhema had welcomed yet another upright Deliverer.

ch: …假如万事顺利，我们也许不会再见。感谢你们为翁法罗斯带来的一切。
en: If all goes well, we may not meet again. Thank you for everything you've brought to Amphoreus.

ch: 如今我身负神权，隐隐能感觉到…你们对翁法罗斯的未来重要非凡。
en: Now that I bear the divine within me, I can get the vague feeling that... that you have a great significance for the future of Amphoreus.

ch: 纷争世以来的漫长岁月，有无数世人、无数眷属、无数黄金裔与我、与奥赫玛、与泰坦们产生了命运的交织。不知你要问哪些人？
en: Over the many long years since the Era Bellica, there have been countless people, Titankins, and Chrysos Heirs who have formed bonds of destiny with myself, Okhema, and the Titans. Which ones are you referring to?

ch: 纷争之新神…万敌！
en: The new god of Strife... Mydei!

ch: 决然接下「天谴之矛」的火种，以不容置喙的王令终结悬锋王朝，又为族人带来荣耀的崭新未来……
en: He decided to accept the Coreflame of the Lance of Fury, ended the dynasty of Kremnos with an unquestionable royal decree, and brought his people a new future of glory...

ch: 如今孤身把守悬锋、将黑潮尽数拦截的迈德漠斯，在众人心目中，已然当得起翁法罗斯守护者一称。
en: Now remaining alone at Castrum Kremnos and holding back the entirety of the black tide, Mydeimos has undoubtedly earned the title of Guardian of Amphoreus in the hearts of the people.

ch: 愚钝的阿那克萨戈拉斯，树庭七贤人之一，从事渎神的「炼金术」研究，行事狂狷不羁、独爱口放厥词。
en: Anaxagoras the fool, one of the Seven Sages of the Grove, who engages in heretical alchemical research and acts with reckless arrogance, loving to spew nonsense with abandon.

ch: 看来，学习施展「百界门」对于小灰来说还是过于勉强了。不过不要难过，小灰很厉害！不需要「百界门」，也一定能成为翁法罗斯的大英雄！
en: It seems that learning to use the "Century Gate" is a bit too difficult for you now, Little Gray. But don't be disheartened. You are already amazing! We know that you will become a great hero of Amphoreus even without the power of the "Century Gate"!

ch: 在*我们*离开雅努萨波利斯后，这座城邦的黑暗愈发深重。继任的圣女遭受了较之*我们*更加严峻的控制…比如，现在生活在奥赫玛的「传谕梦母」。
en: After we left Janusopolis, the darkness that shrouded the city grew heavier. The Holy Maidens who succeeded us endured even stricter control — such as the current "Oracle Dreammother", who resides in Okhema.

ch: 遐蝶如今身在冥界，守候着翁法罗斯的生死流转。现在你之所见，只是复现而出的残像。
en: Castorice is now in the nether realm, guarding the cycle of life and death for Amphoreus. What you see now is simply a reflection, a lingering shadow of the past.

ch: 纷争世以来的漫长岁月，有无数世人、无数眷属、无数黄金裔与我、与奥赫玛、与泰坦们产生了命运的交织。不知你要问哪些人？
en: Over the many long years since the Era Bellica, there have been countless people, Titankins, and Chrysos Heirs who have formed bonds of destiny with myself, Okhema, and the Titans. Which ones are you referring to?

ch: 我会为吾师她们忧虑，曾对万敌心怀忌惮，也总替白厄未来将负的重担而不安。
en: I worry for my teacher and the others, I was once fearful of Mydei, and I am always uneasy about the weight of the burden that Phainon will carry in the future.

ch: 而属于白厄的命运，也已步步趋近…他将如烈阳一般冉冉升起，接下我的职责，为翁法罗斯的人们照亮前路。
en: As for Phainon's destiny, it too approaches step by step... He will rise like a blazing sun, take on my responsibilities, and light the way for the people of Amphoreus.

ch: 可不知从何时起，她突然变得与我们那般疏离，整日在奥赫玛之外漂泊羁旅，连与她面对面道声好都是那么奢侈。
en: But somewhere along the way, she became distant and spent all her days wandering beyond Okhema, and it became a rare luxury just to greet her face-to-face.

ch: 而在翁法罗斯坠入永夜之后…我在树庭重新建立起了昏光庭院。
en: After Amphoreus fell into eternal night... I rebuilt the Twilight Courtyard in the Grove.

ch: 最近我在奥赫玛到处转悠，寻找合适的角落，我想在奥赫玛重新建造一处昏光庭院。
en: Recently, I've been wandering around Okhema, looking for the perfect spot to rebuild the Twilight Courtyard.

ch: 翁法罗斯的医术是什么样的？
en: What is Amphorean healing like?

ch: 原来葫芦还能治病吗…要是树庭还保留着这类植物的种子就好了。
en: So gourds can even be used for healing? If only the Grove still had seeds from plants like that...

ch: 但是万敌却说我并非真正热爱战斗……
en: But Mydei says I don't truly love battle...

ch: 哈…我哪有那么高尚啊。比起我，如今独自在悬锋城的万敌，背负的职责才更沉重吧？
en: Ha... I'm not that noble. Compared to me, Mydei carries far heavier duties alone in Castrum Kremnos, don't you think?

ch: 我没有阿格莱雅的思虑与手腕，没有万敌的信念与心性，没有遐蝶的理智与审慎…而与缇宝老师她们比起来，我还少了一份乐观与执着。
en: I don't have Aglaea's foresight and strategy, Mydei's conviction and resilience, or Castorice's rationality and caution... I even lack Tribbie's optimism and determination.

ch: 可是，我却被伙伴们称作最完美的黄金裔，被奥赫玛的人民奉为救世主。
en: Yet, my companions call me the most perfect Chrysos Heir, and the people of Okhema revere me as the Deliverer.

ch: 关于盗火行者和你的过去……
en: About the Flame Reaver and your past...

ch: 其实还好啦！在见到盗火行者以前，我或许还会被那段阴影所紧缚，毕竟在童年的我看来，那道高大的黑色身影是那样残忍而不可战胜。
en: Honestly, it's not that bad! Before I encountered the Flame Reaver, I might have still been bound by that shadow. After all, as a child, I saw that tall, black figure as cruel and invincible.

ch: 没错。如今，盗火行者站在逐火之旅的对立面，它是奥赫玛的敌人，是所有对「再创世」抱有美好期待的人们的敌人。
en: Exactly. Now, the Flame Reaver stands in opposition to the Flame-Chase Journey. They are the enemy of Okhema and all who hold beautiful hopes for the Era Nova.

ch: 但请你相信我…我的私仇，在翁法罗斯的未来面前，根本微不足道。
en: But trust me... my grudge pales in comparison to Amphoreus's future.

ch: 万敌…真令人刮目相看。
en: Mydei... is truly impressive.

ch: 迈德漠斯，悬锋的新王，他曾一度在奥赫玛收获同伴和认可。但为了世界的明天，他选择离开，投身于永无止境的战争。
en: Mydeimos, the new king of Kremnos, once gained companions and recognition in Okhema. Yet, for the sake of the world's future, he chose to leave and devote himself to endless war.

ch: 袭击树庭的怪人…到底是何方神圣？
en: The strange entity who attacked the Grove... who could they be?

ch: 难道他是黑潮的化身？又或是某位泰坦的孑遗？若非万敌及时继承了「纷争」的神力，那牛皮藓似的家伙怕是要再追杀你们一阵。
en: Could it be an embodiment of the black tide? Or perhaps the remnant of some Titan? If not for Mydei inheriting the divinity of "Strife" in time, that persistent foe might have kept pursuing you.

ch: 首先，我先声明，我不是你理解的「坏人」。我只是树庭结业不久的学生，给元老院做事纯粹是为了丰厚薪酬，绝无任何政治立场。
en: For the record, I'm not who you think I am. I'm just a new graduate from the Grove, working for the Council of Elders purely for the generous pay. I have zero political stance.

ch: 你说你是刚从树庭结业的学生？
en: You mentioned you just graduated from the Grove?

ch: 所以我认清了现实，早早结业，领受父母之命出来找工作了。元老院的工作就是家父推荐来的——也因此，我躲过了树庭惨案。
en: So, I came to terms with reality, graduated early, and followed my parents' advice to start job hunting. My father recommended this position at Council of Elders — which, incidentally, helped me avoid the Grove tragedy.

ch: 哦，这个啊…也没什么可隐瞒的——我是靠奥赫玛之外的黑潮发财的。
en: Oh, that? It's no secret — I made my fortune outside Okhema, in the black tide.

ch: 在我看来，奥赫玛的管理者，无论是元老院、还是黄金裔，都不过徒有其名——每年税金浪费了不少，效率远不如我们这些商人。
en: To me, Okhema's administration — whether it's the Council of Elders or the Chrysos Heirs — is just an empty shell. Every year, so much tax money is wasted with little to show for it. The bureaucrats can't hold a candle to us merchants when it comes to efficiency.

ch: 奥赫玛想要进步，就需要更先进、更实际的社会架构与管理方式。我有心成为这个伟大变革的推动者，奈何我只是个「平民」。
en: If Okhema wants progress, it needs a better, more practical system of governance. I'd love to spearhead that change, but alas, I'm just a "civilian."

ch: 啧…等我改变奥赫玛的行政效率时，你就会知道的。
en: Tch... Well, you'll understand when I've streamlined Okhema's governance.

ch: 我的理想是独立于奥赫玛管理者之外，拆解官僚机构，削减多余监管法规和浪费的开支，让陈腐的管理者早点滚出黎明云崖。
en: My ambition is to create an administration independent of Okhema's rulers — one that dismantles the bureaucracy, slashes excessive regulations, and cuts wasteful spending. It's time to send those antiquated officials packing from Dawncloud.

ch: 啊，这位贵客，请留步——您不是奥赫玛土生土长的住民，对吧？
en: Ah, esteemed guest, please wait a moment — you're not a native of Okhema, are you?

ch: …故乡覆灭时，我正在奥赫玛巡演，无家可归的我，自那以后就定居在了奥赫玛。现在想来，我生活于此的时光早已超过了故乡「米里奥斯」。
en: ...When my homeland fell, I was touring Okhema. Left without a home, I've lived here ever since. Now, I've spent more years in Okhema than I ever did in Milios.

ch: 我的过去啊…要从何说起呢？正如你所知道的，我是米里奥斯人，不是奥赫玛人——不过我定居奥赫玛的起因，可能和大部分外地人不太一样。
en: My experiences, huh... Where should I begin? As you can see, I'm a native of Milios, not Okhema. But the reason I settled here might be quite different from most outsiders.

ch: 本人柯库翁，来自奥凯纳斯，一座以竞技体育闻名于翁法罗斯的古老城邦。
en: I am Glykon of Okeanas. It is an ancient Amphorean city-state known for competitive sports.

ch: 哈哈哈哈，你不会觉得我是在自吹自擂吧？可惜这奥赫玛还没有举办过同等级的大型体育竞技赛事，你不认识我，我不怪你。
en: Hahaha, you don't think I'm tooting my own horn, do you? It's a shame that Okhema has yet to host any large-scale sports competition on the same level, so I don't blame you for not knowing me.

ch: 是「奥凯纳斯运动会」——整个翁法罗斯最著名的运动会！天啊，真的需要我给你解释一遍吗？这翁法罗斯干脆毁灭算了。
en: The Okeanas Games — the most renowned sports event in the entire Amphoreus! Goodness, do I really need to explain it to you again? Amphoreus really is doomed.

ch: 我很怀念桂冠和掌声，圣城也得办奥赫玛运动会才行啊！
en: I miss the laurels and applause very much. The holy city needs its own Okhema Games!

ch: 有位树庭的哲学家想要伸手摘杏子，却被妻子责备，他说「我就是摘了，又*翁法罗斯粗口*会怎么样呢？」片刻之后，他死了。
en: There was a philosopher of the Grove who tried to pick some apricots but was scolded by his wife. He said, "So what if I picked them? What the *Amphoreus profanity* is going to happen?" Moments later, he died.

ch: 我，特阿诺，生前住在奥赫玛，在雅努萨波利斯度过了生命最后的三十年，美好地生，美丽地死。
en: I, Theano, lived in Okhema and spent my final <unbreak>30</unbreak> years in Janusopolis. I lived a good life and died a beautiful death.

ch: 这是留在神悟树庭附近的贤人墓——
en: This is engraved on a sage's tomb near the Grove of Epiphany —

ch: 是这样啊，你是刚到奥赫玛来的外邦遗民吧？这个时节，确实有很多人来寻求庇护。
en: I see... you must be a foreigner who's just arrived in Okhema, right? Many come seeking refuge.

ch: 对了，看你有些面生，打扮也不像是我们这儿的人。你是第一次来吧？这里是奥赫玛最神圣的地方。
en: By the way, you look unfamiliar, and your clothes don't seem like ours. This must be your first time here, yes? This is the holiest place in Okhema.

ch: 嗯…黑潮肆虐，所有人都在寻找一个容身之所，而奥赫玛，或许就是大家最后的希望了。
en: Hmm... The black tide ravages everything, and people are searching for shelter. Okhema, perhaps, is the last hope for all of us.

ch: …这么多年过去了…欢笑，泪滴，奥赫玛的回忆……
en: ...So many years have passed... Laughter and tears are all part of Okhema's memories.

ch: 奥赫玛最近怎么样？
en: How are things in Okhema lately?

ch: 我平日里都在侍奉刻法勒，很少到外面去，因此对奥赫玛其他地方的情况，我了解的不多。
en: I spend most of my days serving Kephale and rarely venture outside, so I know little about the rest of Okhema.

ch: 不过，黑潮无休止地蔓延，最近每天都有新的流民过来。他们在祈祷的时候声音都在抖，我猜想，奥赫玛的整体情况恐怕也不乐观。
en: However, the black tide spreads ceaselessly, and every day brings new refugees. When they pray, even their voices tremble. I'm afraid the overall situation in Okhema is far from hopeful.

ch: 的确，但并非奥赫玛的贵族——奥德里西安的「门瑟」世家，以律法的名义，向你致意。
en: Indeed, but not of Okhema. In the name of the law, House Mentes of Odressian greets you.

ch: 彼时黑潮压境，家族直系恐怕只剩我一人了。举城迁来奥赫玛时，我护住一人，又被黄金裔阁下所救，那时我才理解，何为「贵族的荣耀」。
en: With the black tide at our doorsteps, I'm afraid I am the only surviving heir of my family. It was during the great migration to Okhema, when I sheltered another and was in turn rescued by the Chrysos Heir, that I finally understood the meaning of a noble's honor.

ch: 这便是贵族的规则。但说白了，因人而异罢了。即使是奥赫玛，也有着严重缺乏身为贵族自知的元老。他们也该被丢到黑潮里洗一洗，说不定就好了。
en: These are the rules of nobility. But in reality, it all depends on the individual. Even in Okhema, there are elders who sorely lack the self-awareness befitting a noble. Perhaps a thorough rinse in the black tide would do them good.

ch: 你在奥赫玛这里是做什么的？
en: What are you doing in Okhema?

ch: 履行贵族义务，参与议会的讨论，为城邦与民众谋求长存，我为此职责深感自豪。可惜，我的一票与民众的一票相当，奥赫玛还是太过保守了。
en: Performing my noble duties, participating in council discussions, and seeking long-term solutions for the survival of the city-state and its people. I take great pride in this responsibility, but alas, my vote is equal to any other. Okhema remains overly conservative.

ch: 起初，他们说{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}会庇佑卡弥特斯不陷落于黑潮。然后，他们说奥赫玛会永远不受黑夜侵袭……
en: At first, they claimed {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#} would protect Carmitis from the black tide. Then, they promised Okhema would never be touched by the dark night...

ch: …嗯，这都是借口。云石天宫承载着奥赫玛人的热情与回忆，它可能会打烊，但却从未冷清过。我眷恋这片水流，所以我会留在这。这…是我的固执。
en: Well, I'm just making excuses. The Marmoreal Palace carries the passion and memories of the Okhemans. It may close temporarily, but it has never been desolate. I'm attached to these waters, so I'll stay here. Call it... stubborn, I don't know.

ch: 嗯…我在树庭研习多年，又把大半辈子花在了研究上。仰赖瑟希斯赋予的理性，在我看来，那刻夏的论证并无纰漏。
en: Hmm... I studied at the Grove for many years and spent most of my life doing research. By the wisdom bestowed by Cerces, I see no flaws in Anaxa's reasoning.

ch: 我的家人在黑潮中不幸身亡，我逃来奥赫玛投靠亲戚时，马尔库斯就在我们附近的街上晃悠。
en: My family died in the black tide, and when I fled to Okhema to stay with relatives, Marcus was always hanging around the street nearby.

ch: 相信我，朋友，这地方可是整个奧赫玛最好的席位——无论是见证神明的谢幕，还是观看凡人的演出。
en: Trust me, my friend, this spot offers the best view in all of Okhema — whether witnessing a deity's final act or observing mortal theatrics.

ch: 奥赫玛是我们最后要守护、也最终会庇护我们的家园，一定是的。
en: Okhema is the last home we'll protect... and will ultimately shelter us. It must be.

ch: 想到了翁法罗斯可能根本没有未来？那的确非常可怕…不过，我们也可以试着把它变成生存的动力。
en: Frightened by the possibility that Amphoreus has no future? It is terrifying... but perhaps we can transform that fear into a will to survive.

ch: 翁法罗斯人的灵魂是不灭的吗？很可能。
en: Are the souls of Amphoreans immortal? Quite possibly.

ch: 不过，多亏了阁下的援手，现在安全区的范围有所扩大，我们也能得到更多喘息的空间。翁法罗斯迎来新生前，一切还是不能大意。
en: However, thanks to your help, the safe zones have expanded somewhat, giving us more breathing room. Still, we mustn't lower our guard until Amphoreus is reborn.

ch: 万敌他……
en: And Mydei...

ch: 不死的迈德漠斯牺牲了——目睹了那一幕的圣城卫士已为我送来了悲报。
en: The undying Mydeimos has fallen — Holy City Guards who witnessed his end have delivered this tragic news to me.

ch: 阁下，收起你的哀容吧——迈德漠斯必不愿见此。
en: Please, cast aside your grief — Mydeimos would not want you to grieve.

ch: 但愿再创世后，他们真的能够站上奥赫玛小贩业的顶点。到那时候，你也一定要去看看「奥凯纳斯运动会」的盛况啊！
en: I hope that after Era Nova, they truly rise to become the finest merchants in Okhema. When that happens, you absolutely must witness the grandeur of the Okeanas Games!

ch: 如今，末日的舞台被搬到奥赫玛…若我还有时间，我该如何为后人传递文明的信号？
en: And now, Okhema has become the stage of the apocalypse... If I still have time, how should I leave a message for those who come after us?

ch: 正如我们初见时我所说，我无比期盼翁法罗斯的明天……
en: As I told you when we first met, I long for Amphoreus's tomorrow...

ch: 但，连永不熄灭的黎明机器都撑不住了，我看这就是翁法罗斯的终点了……
en: But even the ever-burning Dawn Device can't hold up anymore. I think this is the end of Amphoreus...

ch: 嗯…有位织者醉心于黄金血的甜美，就像悬锋战士沉迷于蜜酿。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>褔坛的金池中央</color>，希望逃遁进另一个世界呢。
en: Hmm... There is a weaver who is mesmerized by the sweetness of Golden Blood, like a Kremnos Warrior addicted to honey brew. The golden thread brings tidings: They now reside <color=#dbc291ff>in the center of the Golden Pond at the Altar of Blessings</color>, hoping to escape into another world.

ch: 贵客早已知晓黑潮对翁法罗斯大地的摧残，无论人还是奇兽，都无法从它的影响下幸存——唯有一物例外。
en: The esteemed guest is already aware of the devastation the black tide has wrought upon the land of Amphoreus. Neither humans nor strange beasts can survive its influence, only one thing is an exception.

ch: 这，便是从无到有的第一步。若能探明织者之秘背后的原理，终有一天，翁法罗斯的人民将不再畏惧天灾。
en: This is the first step from nothing to something. If we can uncover the secrets behind the weavers' mystery, one day, the people of Amphoreus will no longer fear natural disasters.

ch: 行吧，听你的。臭小子，那会儿连个锄头都举不起来，一转眼就要去悬锋城学剑了……
en: Alright, have it your way. Kid, not long ago you couldn't even lift a hoe, and now you're off to Castrum Kremnos to train with a sword...

ch: 哀丽秘榭要是没了这片麦田…都不知道要怎么数季节呢。
en: If Aedes Elysiae ever loses this wheat field... I don't even know how we'd keep track of the seasons.

ch: 你之前说…是要去悬锋城，对吧？不知道那里离咱们的哀丽秘榭有多远？
en: You said... you're going to Castrum Kremnos, right? I wonder how far that is from our Aedes Elysiae?

ch: 哈哈，我也不知道。听说悬锋城像大地兽一样，会慢慢到处走动呢。
